| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bc7bd1b2-483c-45ee-9e37-2d112a53fd73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Enough of a 2024 PTSD trigger for ya?
Say what you want about James Carville, but he was right when he coined that phrase for Bill Clinton's first presidential campaign. It's a lesson Democrats keep forgetting.
When people vote, it almost always comes down to 4 or 5 priorities:
* Putting food on the table
* Paying for housing and keeping the lights on
* Staying healthy without medical debt
* Making sure their kids get a good education
* Getting to work on time

All of these are economic issues. Even the issues that _don 't appear economic on the surface_ are tied back to the economy.
**You want to talk about safety?** Without jobs and opportunity, schools fail, crime rises, and food deserts form.
**You want to talk about immigration?** Deporting workers shrinks the workforce, raises food and construction costs, and slashes tax revenue.
**You want to talk about the environment?** Dirty air and water drive up healthcare costs and lower productivity. Climate change disrupts food supply and spikes grocery prices. Transitioning to clean energy requires affordable EVs and reliable transit.
The list goes on. But it all comes back to one thing: the economy.
Don't mistake this for a "Third Way" revival. Quoting Carville doesn't mean politicking like him. This isn't about running to the middle and punching left. It's about getting back to our Democratic roots: fighting for everyday Americans against billionaires who profit from their struggles--without compromising our values.
If we return to campaigning on the basics--home, bills, food, health, schools, and work--we win.
## **Voters Vote with their Wallets.**
Voters don't always vote with their hearts or even their brains. They vote with their wallets and their pocketbooks. And election after election, the economy tops the list:
* **[2024 Presidential Election:](https://substack.com/redirect/66021ae5-a1b9-48f3-b427-1cc7e05e7b67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.
* **[2022 Midterms:](https://substack.com/redirect/ba80491b-2876-4b69-9e57-c6860aa6e0d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.
* **[2020 Presidential Election:](https://substack.com/redirect/8c4a5b7e-f255-4eb9-8f12-47987405c3d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.
* **[2018 Midterms:](https://substack.com/redirect/7dd0ef7c-ac95-44bd-974e-af88c1241da3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** healthcare was #1, but the economy came right after.
* **[2016 Presidential Election:](https://substack.com/redirect/c53b9760-3c57-48ae-8d2d-32e12b937a9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.

You get the general idea. Cycle after cycle, it's been right there in front of us.
And yet, after every loss, strategists and pundits scratch their heads and ask: _" Was it because of abortion? Was it because she was a woman? Was it because we didn't talk enough about trans kids in schools?"_
**F** ***ING NO. The voters have been telling us what matters every single time--it's the economy.
And here's the kicker: it's not like voters are against us on social issues. In fact, most Americans agree with Democrats on nearly all of them:
* [63% believe](https://substack.com/redirect/fc399899-6215-4d43-9060-2ff99814600b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) abortion should be legal.
* [68% support ](https://substack.com/redirect/881a9ad4-6dbb-4b26-b452-19d0778292c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)marriage equality and LGBTQ rights.
* [58% favor](https://substack.com/redirect/64806504-9a2b-4894-a4b4-cca1e90fc9f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) stricter gun laws.
* [61% say](https://substack.com/redirect/1b4845ae-6581-4e41-b27c-7bc37666b98a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) climate change is a serious problem.
* [68% approve ](https://substack.com/redirect/f30d4e6a-2565-4718-932f-4acec480aa87?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)of labor unions (the highest in 60 years).
* [81% believe](https://substack.com/redirect/be0d9990-4d53-47d1-b116-5ef07e69e604?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the criminal justice system needs reform.

These aren't controversial positions. The real divide isn't on social issues--it's on whether people feel like they can afford groceries, rent, gas, or healthcare. That's where elections are won and lost.
When it comes to voters' issues, the economy is king. And the state of the economy--or more importantly, how people _perceive_ the state of the economy--will dictate how they vote, especially in presidential years.
This isn't new. History has been screaming it at us:
[In 2008](https://substack.com/redirect/3ac9bef8-adb6-47bb-9023-750398ca3276?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), America was in the midst of the Great Recession. Voters blamed George Bush and Republicans, and they chose Barack Obama.
[In 2016,](https://substack.com/redirect/4777ba52-f35e-4a35-b446-3ca7a441af7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) plenty of voters still felt left behind after eight years of Obama. They wanted change and rolled the dice on Donald Trump.
[In 2020](https://substack.com/redirect/b3f66c83-9cca-4ba7-9e40-ecd1e0d26cf7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Trump's disastrous handling of the economy during COVID sank him, so voters turned back to Joe Biden.
[In 2024,](https://substack.com/redirect/a3e5a22a-c875-46db-8f49-e22bb3281aa3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) people were feeling the crunch from inflation and rising costs. Whether the numbers said things were improving didn't matter--public perception said otherwise. And Biden and Harris paid the price.
## **Social Justice and Equality, But Make It Different**
What also matters is how Democrats are _perceived_ when it comes to handling the economy. And unfortunately, cycle after cycle, Democrats get cast as the party that doesn't understand bread-and-butter economics.
In 2024, voters said Trump would handle the economy better than Kamala Harris--54% to 45%. We all know how that ended.
[A recent poll by the Searchlight Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/2eae284d-890b-4ceb-b8ea-c788a5430b71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) asked voters in battleground states what issues leaders should focus on: affordability, healthcare, and jobs. The same poll asked what issues voters think Democrats are focused on: climate change, LGBTQ+ issues, and healthcare. [The New York Times echoed](https://substack.com/redirect/d92f89db-c094-4a00-8864-e31097cb5417?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the same thing earlier this year. The result? Voters conclude Democrats don't share their priorities.
And that's on us. For years, Democrats' biggest rallying cry has been "saving democracy." Don't get me wrong--it's a noble cause. But when people can't afford groceries, when rent is eating half their paycheck, when medical debt is crushing them, talk of authoritarianism or "the orangeman in the White House" doesn't land. It's not that they don't care--it's that survival comes first.
Here's the truth: **economic justice is social justice.** Equity isn't just about identity--it's about making sure people can afford a home, have healthcare that won't bankrupt them, and send their kids to good schools. Standing up for unions and organizing for workers is fighting for equity. Raising wages and lowering costs is fighting for equity. Yet Democrats too often silo these struggles as separate lanes.
The irony is, we already have the moral high ground. We're the ones fighting for the working class. Republicans are very open about the fact that they legislate for billionaires and their donors. And yet, because we don't tell the story well enough, we still lose the "economy" perception war.
If Democrats want to save democracy, we have to connect it directly to the economy. _**Not just what we 're saving it from--but what we're saving it for.**_
That's the difference between being on defense and actually giving voters a reason to turn out. When we focus only on warning about authoritarianism, we sound like we're asking people to vote out of fear. When we connect democracy to lowering grocery bills, keeping people in their homes, protecting unions, and building an economy that works for everyone, we give them something to fight for.
**Because we need to give voters something to vote for, not just something to vote against.**
The closest Democrats have come to recentering the economy in recent years has been through the _Fighting Oligarchy_ tour with Senator Bernie Sanders and Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. Together, they've gone into red and purple districts that too many Democrats write off, hammering on affordability, union power, and the corrosive influence of billionaires.
What's striking is not just their message--it's their audience. They've drawn large, diverse crowds in areas represented by Republicans who haven't lifted a finger for working families. Instead of talking past people, they've spoken to the pain of rising grocery bills, unaffordable housing, and medical debt. They've shown voters that _Democrats can be the party of the working class again,_ not just the party warning about Trump.
That's the blueprint: speak directly to material struggles, tie them to corporate greed and billionaire dominance, and show how progressive economic policy improves people's daily lives.
Because here's the bottom line: voters know Republicans are fighting for billionaires. If we don't prove--loudly and clearly--that Democrats are fighting for them, we'll keep losing ground. And instead of chasing the Right's talking points, we need to double down on the economic fights that actually win people's trust.
### **Don 't play down to their level.**
One flag I want to reiterate is this: in 2024, instead of focusing on the issues voters told us were the most important, too many Democrats decided to chase voters who were never on our side to begin with.
Representatives like **[Seth Moulton](https://substack.com/redirect/58312c07-19fa-45a0-83eb-6b6b4fca3a76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and **[Tom Suozzi](https://substack.com/redirect/3391049d-7f02-43ac-a4c2-bcac42514e96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** were quick to scapegoat marginalized groups like trans kids in sports as the reason Democrats lost ground. Others went even further.
[Forty-six Democrats in the House](https://substack.com/redirect/b222c97c-b57f-426e-b4e7-7503dda1ef10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [eleven in the Senate](https://substack.com/redirect/ece21e6d-d220-4863-a278-192ae3a0e6a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) voted for the flawed **Laken Riley Act** , a bill that demonizes immigrants and trashes due process. That single vote helped pave the way for Trump's ICE-led mass deportations.
[Thirty-one House Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/c8c84139-ee3a-43e5-a50d-be5f058d85e3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) even voted to give Trump and Republicans full control of sentencing laws in the District of Columbia--this after many had publicly argued that crime in DC wasn't nearly as bad as Fox News was making it out to be. Their votes weren't about solving problems; they were about fear and expediency.
These betrayals sent a clear message to the base: instead of fighting for you, we'll throw you under the bus if we think it'll win us a couple of "swing voters" who were never with us in the first place.
Let's be blunt: if the number one issue keeping someone from voting for Kamala Harris was **transgender athletes** , they were _never_ going to vote for us. And yet, we wasted precious oxygen and political capital pandering to these voters, while ignoring the ones who actually could have made the difference--the disaffected millions who sat out the election because they feel both parties have abandoned them and that both parties are the same.
And while I reject the premise, I understand the frustration.
That's the tragedy. Because every time Democrats echo right-wing frames or cave on issues of justice, we reinforce the very narrative that fuels the "both sides are the same" cynicism. And that cynicism is poison. It keeps working families, young people, and communities of color--our base--from showing up
That's why the campaigns that are cutting through are the ones centering kitchen table economics. Rep. Mikie Sherrill rolled out a ["Save You Time and Money"](https://substack.com/redirect/99a8d466-9df4-4170-ab7a-3917bd11d800?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) agenda in her New Jersey gubernatorial bid, hammering affordability as her north star. State Senator Zohran Mamdani has put economic justice at the center of [his New York City Mayoral platform](https://substack.com/redirect/3547b38c-6d5c-4370-81bd-5ee74506ccbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by emphasizing housing affordability, grocery prices, and childcare costs. And here in Virginia, Rep. Abigail Spanberger launched her ["Affordable Virginia"](https://substack.com/redirect/621fb3e7-16bd-4002-a459-48958e7ce647?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) plan for governor--[contrasting her agenda for lower costs](https://substack.com/redirect/a39d1fa6-6615-4090-b80a-ba7ad1776a93?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with Lt. Governor Winsome Sears' record of supporting Trump's Big Beautiful Bill, which raised costs for everyday Americans.
And how has Sears responded? With ads about...trans kids in schools and trans athletes.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/17dfcaa1-43cb-4244-9a88-23fe35b01a31?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is the contrast. One side is offering voters real solutions to make their lives easier and more affordable. The other side is obsessing over culture war fights that have nothing to do with your rent, your grocery bill, or your paycheck.
The answer isn't to play down to their level. It's to raise the stakes back up--to show voters that Democrats stand for them, not against them.
## **The Blueprint For Us, Not Just Against Them**
I have said it before, and I will say it again: we need to give the voters a reason to vote for us and not just against them.
We don't have to reinvent the wheel. Democrats already have the blueprint for how to win, and it dates back nearly a century. Franklin Delano Roosevelt showed us exactly what happens when a party is bold enough to take on concentrated wealth and put working families first. His New Deal didn't just provide relief in the darkest days of the Depression -- it rebuilt faith in government, created millions of jobs, and gave people a reason to believe that politics could change their lives for the better.
Every time Democrats have remembered that lesson, we've built durable coalitions and made progress. When we've drifted away from it, we've lost ground. The War on Poverty, Medicare, Social Security, and the expansion of civil rights all grew from the idea that government should be on the side of ordinary people. But in the decades since, too many Democrats bought into austerity politics and Wall Street talking points, drifting toward centrist triangulation instead of doubling down on the bread-and-butter fights that actually win elections.
Republicans have no shame in telling us outright that their agenda is to serve the billionaire class. That's their side of the fight. Our side has to be clear and unapologetic about ours: to serve working people. Economic justice is social justice. Affordable housing, strong unions, good schools, and access to healthcare aren't just policies -- they're the building blocks of a fair society. And Democrats have the legacy, the record, and the moral high ground to make that case.
This isn't just a national strategy--it's already happening on the ground. In Virginia, Delegate Joshua Cole has made his campaign merch all about "affordable housing, good-paying jobs, excellent schools, & affordable prescriptions." [Delegate Michael Feggans has leaned ](https://substack.com/redirect/6246011d-c850-4840-be17-c7a991a627a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)into his veteran background, pointing out how Trump's cuts hurt Virginia's workforce, veterans' healthcare, and military families in his district. And Lily Franklin, running in one of the most competitive districts in the Commonwealth, pushed back on attacks about her working-class roots by embracing them--[showing in her ads](https://substack.com/redirect/489eb55b-54b7-4132-a106-28aa6f5f4ac7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that she is just like so many voters she hopes to represent.
These aren't side battles; they're the fight. And if Democrats stick to them, we win.
Because at the end of the day, voters don't want culture war nonsense. They don't want scapegoats. They want to know how they're going to pay for groceries, keep the lights on, see a doctor, and give their kids a future.
Republicans are handing us the playbook when they rant about trans kids or demonize immigrants. They're telling us they've got nothing real to offer. That's our opening. We don't need to match them punch for punch on outrage--we need to hammer home that Democrats are the ones fighting for your paycheck, your health, your kids, and your home.
So here's the bottom line:**It 's the economy, damn it. It's always been the economy. And the party that remembers that will be the party that wins.**
**And the message is that no matter who you are, where you 're from, who you love, how you worship, what your family looks like: you deserve to get ahead and not just get by. Period. It's that simple.**
**And if the economy you are selling isn 't working for voters--like we tried to do in 2024--they won't buy it.**
### **TL;DR**
Democrats keep overthinking elections, but the truth has never changed: it's the economy, stupid. Voters consistently rank affordability, healthcare, and jobs as their top issues--yet Democrats too often focus on abstract ideas like "saving democracy" or get dragged into right-wing culture wars.
Cycle after cycle, public perception of who handles the economy better has swung elections--from Obama in 2008, to Trump in 2016, to Biden in 2020, and back to Trump in 2024. If people feel their wallets are hurting, they'll vote for change--no matter who's in charge.
Social issues still matter, but most Americans already side with Democrats on abortion rights, LGBTQ equality, unions, and climate action. What they want is a party that connects those fights back to their daily lives: lower grocery bills, affordable housing, decent healthcare, and good jobs.
When Democrats lead with pocketbook issues--as Bernie Sanders, AOC, Joshua Cole, Michael Feggans, Lily Franklin, and others have--voters respond. When they chase right-wing frames or scapegoat marginalized groups, they lose.
The blueprint exists: FDR's New Deal, working-class populism, and campaigns rooted in economic justice. To win, Democrats don't just need to run _against_ Trump--they need to give people something to run _for_.
**Bottom line: it 's still the economy, damn it--and whoever remembers that first will win.**
## SIDE BAR

Early voting has already begun and goes until November 1st. This is the first real test of Democratic resilience during the 2nd Trump Administratio,n and as they say, as goes Virginia, so goes the nation!

clean_body: and it's completely different, but it's still the economy | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? and it's completely different, but it's still the economy | | | Oct 20 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Enough of a 2024 PTSD trigger for ya? Say what you want about James Carville, but he was right when he coined that phrase for Bill Clinton's first presidential campaign. It's a lesson Democrats keep forgetting. When people vote, it almost always comes down to 4 or 5 priorities: Putting food on the table Paying for housing and keeping the lights on Staying healthy without medical debt Making sure their kids get a good education Getting to work on time All of these are economic issues. Even the issues that don 't appear economic on the surface are tied back to the economy. You want to talk about safety? Without jobs and opportunity, schools fail, crime rises, and food deserts form. You want to talk about immigration? Deporting workers shrinks the workforce, raises food and construction costs, and slashes tax revenue. You want to talk about the environment? Dirty air and water drive up healthcare costs and lower productivity. Climate change disrupts food supply and spikes grocery prices. Transitioning to clean energy requires affordable EVs and reliable transit. The list goes on. But it all comes back to one thing: the economy. Don't mistake this for a "Third Way" revival. Quoting Carville doesn't mean politicking like him. This isn't about running to the middle and punching left. It's about getting back to our Democratic roots: fighting for everyday Americans against billionaires who profit from their struggles--without compromising our values. If we return to campaigning on the basics--home, bills, food, health, schools, and work--we win. Voters Vote with their Wallets. Voters don't always vote with their hearts or even their brains. They vote with their wallets and their pocketbooks. And election after election, the economy tops the list: the economy was the most critical issue. the economy was the most critical issue. the economy was the most critical issue. healthcare was #1, but the economy came right after. the economy was the most critical issue. You get the general idea. Cycle after cycle, it's been right there in front of us. And yet, after every loss, strategists and pundits scratch their heads and ask: " Was it because of abortion? Was it because she was a woman? Was it because we didn't talk enough about trans kids in schools?" F *ING NO. The voters have been telling us what matters every single time--it's the economy. And here's the kicker: it's not like voters are against us on social issues. In fact, most Americans agree with Democrats on nearly all of them: abortion should be legal. equality and LGBTQ rights. stricter gun laws. climate change is a serious problem. labor unions (the highest in 60 years). the criminal justice system needs reform. These aren't controversial positions. The real divide isn't on social issues--it's on whether people feel like they can afford groceries, rent, gas, or healthcare. That's where elections are won and lost. When it comes to voters' issues, the economy is king. And the state of the economy--or more importantly, how people perceive the state of the economy--will dictate how they vote, especially in presidential years. This isn't new. History has been screaming it at us: , America was in the midst of the Great Recession. Voters blamed George Bush and Republicans, and they chose Barack Obama. plenty of voters still felt left behind after eight years of Obama. They wanted change and rolled the dice on Donald Trump. , Trump's disastrous handling of the economy during COVID sank him, so voters turned back to Joe Biden. people were feeling the crunch from inflation and rising costs. Whether the numbers said things were improving didn't matter--public perception said otherwise. And Biden and Harris paid the price. Social Justice and Equality, But Make It Different What also matters is how Democrats are perceived when it comes to handling the economy. And unfortunately, cycle after cycle, Democrats get cast as the party that doesn't understand bread-and-butter economics. In 2024, voters said Trump would handle the economy better than Kamala Harris--54% to 45%. We all know how that ended. asked voters in battleground states what issues leaders should focus on: affordability, healthcare, and jobs. The same poll asked what issues voters think Democrats are focused on: climate change, LGBTQ+ issues, and healthcare. the same thing earlier this year. The result? Voters conclude Democrats don't share their priorities. And that's on us. For years, Democrats' biggest rallying cry has been "saving democracy." Don't get me wrong--it's a noble cause. But when people can't afford groceries, when rent is eating half their paycheck, when medical debt is crushing them, talk of authoritarianism or "the orangeman in the White House" doesn't land. It's not that they don't care--it's that survival comes first. Here's the truth: economic justice is social justice. Equity isn't just about identity--it's about making sure people can afford a home, have healthcare that won't bankrupt them, and send their kids to good schools. Standing up for unions and organizing for workers is fighting for equity. Raising wages and lowering costs is fighting for equity. Yet Democrats too often silo these struggles as separate lanes. The irony is, we already have the moral high ground. We're the ones fighting for the working class. Republicans are very open about the fact that they legislate for billionaires and their donors. And yet, because we don't tell the story well enough, we still lose the "economy" perception war. If Democrats want to save democracy, we have to connect it directly to the economy. Not just what we 're saving it from--but what we're saving it for. That's the difference between being on defense and actually giving voters a reason to turn out. When we focus only on warning about authoritarianism, we sound like we're asking people to vote out of fear. When we connect democracy to lowering grocery bills, keeping people in their homes, protecting unions, and building an economy that works for everyone, we give them something to fight for. Because we need to give voters something to vote for, not just something to vote against. The closest Democrats have come to recentering the economy in recent years has been through the Fighting Oligarchy tour with Senator Bernie Sanders and Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. Together, they've gone into red and purple districts that too many Democrats write off, hammering on affordability, union power, and the corrosive influence of billionaires. What's striking is not just their message--it's their audience. They've drawn large, diverse crowds in areas represented by Republicans who haven't lifted a finger for working families. Instead of talking past people, they've spoken to the pain of rising grocery bills, unaffordable housing, and medical debt. They've shown voters that Democrats can be the party of the working class again, not just the party warning about Trump. That's the blueprint: speak directly to material struggles, tie them to corporate greed and billionaire dominance, and show how progressive economic policy improves people's daily lives. Because here's the bottom line: voters know Republicans are fighting for billionaires. If we don't prove--loudly and clearly--that Democrats are fighting for them, we'll keep losing ground. And instead of chasing the Right's talking points, we need to double down on the economic fights that actually win people's trust. Don 't play down to their level. One flag I want to reiterate is this: in 2024, instead of focusing on the issues voters told us were the most important, too many Democrats decided to chase voters who were never on our side to begin with. Representatives like and were quick to scapegoat marginalized groups like trans kids in sports as the reason Democrats lost ground. Others went even further. and voted for the flawed Laken Riley Act , a bill that demonizes immigrants and trashes due process. That single vote helped pave the way for Trump's ICE-led mass deportations. even voted to give Trump and Republicans full control of sentencing laws in the District of Columbia--this after many had publicly argued that crime in DC wasn't nearly as bad as Fox News was making it out to be. Their votes weren't about solving problems; they were about fear and expediency. These betrayals sent a clear message to the base: instead of fighting for you, we'll throw you under the bus if we think it'll win us a couple of "swing voters" who were never with us in the first place. Let's be blunt: if the number one issue keeping someone from voting for Kamala Harris was transgender athletes , they were never going to vote for us. And yet, we wasted precious oxygen and political capital pandering to these voters, while ignoring the ones who actually could have made the difference--the disaffected millions who sat out the election because they feel both parties have abandoned them and that both parties are the same. And while I reject the premise, I understand the frustration. That's the tragedy. Because every time Democrats echo right-wing frames or cave on issues of justice, we reinforce the very narrative that fuels the "both sides are the same" cynicism. And that cynicism is poison. It keeps working families, young people, and communities of color--our base--from showing up That's why the campaigns that are cutting through are the ones centering kitchen table economics. Rep. Mikie Sherrill rolled out a agenda in her New Jersey gubernatorial bid, hammering affordability as her north star. State Senator Zohran Mamdani has put economic justice at the center of by emphasizing housing affordability, grocery prices, and childcare costs. And here in Virginia, Rep. Abigail Spanberger launched her plan for governor-- with Lt. Governor Winsome Sears' record of supporting Trump's Big Beautiful Bill, which raised costs for everyday Americans. And how has Sears responded? With ads about...trans kids in schools and trans athletes. | | ---|---|--- This is the contrast. One side is offering voters real solutions to make their lives easier and more affordable. The other side is obsessing over culture war fights that have nothing to do with your rent, your grocery bill, or your paycheck. The answer isn't to play down to their level. It's to raise the stakes back up--to show voters that Democrats stand for them, not against them. The Blueprint For Us, Not Just Against Them I have said it before, and I will say it again: we need to give the voters a reason to vote for us and not just against them. We don't have to reinvent the wheel. Democrats already have the blueprint for how to win, and it dates back nearly a century. Franklin Delano Roosevelt showed us exactly what happens when a party is bold enough to take on concentrated wealth and put working families first. His New Deal didn't just provide relief in the darkest days of the Depression -- it rebuilt faith in government, created millions of jobs, and gave people a reason to believe that politics could change their lives for the better. Every time Democrats have remembered that lesson, we've built durable coalitions and made progress. When we've drifted away from it, we've lost ground. The War on Poverty, Medicare, Social Security, and the expansion of civil rights all grew from the idea that government should be on the side of ordinary people. But in the decades since, too many Democrats bought into austerity politics and Wall Street talking points, drifting toward centrist triangulation instead of doubling down on the bread-and-butter fights that actually win elections. Republicans have no shame in telling us outright that their agenda is to serve the billionaire class. That's their side of the fight. Our side has to be clear and unapologetic about ours: to serve working people. Economic justice is social justice. Affordable housing, strong unions, good schools, and access to healthcare aren't just policies -- they're the building blocks of a fair society. And Democrats have the legacy, the record, and the moral high ground to make that case. This isn't just a national strategy--it's already happening on the ground. In Virginia, Delegate Joshua Cole has made his campaign merch all about "affordable housing, good-paying jobs, excellent schools, & affordable prescriptions." his veteran background, pointing out how Trump's cuts hurt Virginia's workforce, veterans' healthcare, and military families in his district. And Lily Franklin, running in one of the most competitive districts in the Commonwealth, pushed back on attacks about her working-class roots by embracing them-- that she is just like so many voters she hopes to represent. These aren't side battles; they're the fight. And if Democrats stick to them, we win. Because at the end of the day, voters don't want culture war nonsense. They don't want scapegoats. They want to know how they're going to pay for groceries, keep the lights on, see a doctor, and give their kids a future. Republicans are handing us the playbook when they rant about trans kids or demonize immigrants. They're telling us they've got nothing real to offer. That's our opening. We don't need to match them punch for punch on outrage--we need to hammer home that Democrats are the ones fighting for your paycheck, your health, your kids, and your home. So here's the bottom line:It 's the economy, damn it. It's always been the economy. And the party that remembers that will be the party that wins. And the message is that no matter who you are, where you 're from, who you love, how you worship, what your family looks like: you deserve to get ahead and not just get by. Period. It's that simple. And if the economy you are selling isn 't working for voters--like we tried to do in 2024--they won't buy it. TL;DR Democrats keep overthinking elections, but the truth has never changed: it's the economy, stupid. Voters consistently rank affordability, healthcare, and jobs as their top issues--yet Democrats too often focus on abstract ideas like "saving democracy" or get dragged into right-wing culture wars. Cycle after cycle, public perception of who handles the economy better has swung elections--from Obama in 2008, to Trump in 2016, to Biden in 2020, and back to Trump in 2024. If people feel their wallets are hurting, they'll vote for change--no matter who's in charge. Social issues still matter, but most Americans already side with Democrats on abortion rights, LGBTQ equality, unions, and climate action. What they want is a party that connects those fights back to their daily lives: lower grocery bills, affordable housing, decent healthcare, and good jobs. When Democrats lead with pocketbook issues--as Bernie Sanders, AOC, Joshua Cole, Michael Feggans, Lily Franklin, and others have--voters respond. When they chase right-wing frames or scapegoat marginalized groups, they lose. The blueprint exists: FDR's New Deal, working-class populism, and campaigns rooted in economic justice. To win, Democrats don't just need to run against Trump--they need to give people something to run for. Bottom line: it 's still the economy, damn it--and whoever remembers that first will win. SIDE BAR If you live in the Commonwealth of Virginia, get out and vote before November 4th for our Democratic Nominees for Governor, Lt. Governor, Attorney General, and your House of Delegates District! Early voting has already begun and goes until November 1st. This is the first real test of Democratic resilience during the 2nd Trump Administratio,n and as they say, as goes Virginia, so goes the nation! To find your local early voting location, drop off sites, or your polling place and your candidates: go to iwillvote.com for more information! Thanks for reading By the Ballot! What is By the Ballot?** By the Ballot is a free SubStack that features unapologetic dispatches from the New South 's next generation of Democrats. Commentary on stances, tactics, and news on the Left. We don't claim to know everything or always be right, we just want to be a part of the conversation. While there 's many different ways to power and the Right wants to take over our country by hook or by crook, we want to help Democrats help Americans by making a government for the people by the ballot. subject: How Boebert hopes to rig the 2026 midterms
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
For Democrats to win in 2026, we need to counteract the seats we are losing to MAGA’s gerrymandering by going on the offensive in vulnerable districts like Lauren Boebert.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ylo8_w4qHysT3UDvjae5JdcmP6U0LhfU8qWpb_nTsOfq8YR_VExNdIasJPPmIkh7Cvg3TK9_rhXj7D2zCxsVvfMgAejkCDXuKbsdrRXWrrA_A0OAri26JFKlqLNfYZYwrHalwDaEVUHBevpUjksF7CsdMbTXYJD4hjNj8j5b4OT6Z8NPwEprFdX-ax_J2WCQNjqREIKIO5-JgAommPbiyOzDpQVmPGnF&c=GaFXefLVX8qdv_idC56BtK70BqEuvBN4dcQ1Erfr3aksATPsRak7dw==&ch=VA0JMYDQk-hYfa2qmyv54Zsk8OWulVvAOEzIQVqFsAKcwMmLmc0m6Q==)
---
|

Peter,
Lauren Boebert is spearheading MAGA's efforts to strip millions of their ability to vote ahead of the upcoming '26 midterm elections.
Earlier this year, she wrote and introduced the SAVE ACT--a bill that she has tried to spin as "election security" but in reality is carte blanche for historic levels of voter suppression.
This bill would make it harder for deployed service members, veterans, rural voters, and even women who took their spouse's last name (like Lauren Boebert) to cast a ballot.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ylo8_w4qHysT3UDvjae5JdcmP6U0LhfU8qWpb_nTsOfq8YR_VExNdIasJPPmIkh7Cvg3TK9_rhXj7D2zCxsVvfMgAejkCDXuKbsdrRXWrrA_A0OAri26JFKlqLNfYZYwrHalwDaEVUHBevpUjksF7CsdMbTXYJD4hjNj8j5b4OT6Z8NPwEprFdX-ax_J2WCQNjqREIKIO5_7qTk_RTXJPE3UN-kbQC4F&c=GaFXefLVX8qdv_idC56BtK70BqEuvBN4dcQ1Erfr3aksATPsRak7dw==&ch=VA0JMYDQk-hYfa2qmyv54Zsk8OWulVvAOEzIQVqFsAKcwMmLmc0m6Q==)
---
Boebert's bill is a foundational component of MAGA's strategy to suppress Democrat votes and redraw congressional maps in a desperate attempt to rig the outcome of the 2026 midterms.
For Democrats to win in 2026, we need to counteract the seats we are losing to MAGA's gerrymandering by going on the offensive in vulnerable districts like Lauren Boebert's.
We have seen record-breaking swings towards Democrats in red-leaning districts in elections across the country this year. CO-4 is only a R+9 district, well within the 10-15 point Democratic swings we have witnessed in elections throughout 2025.
Trisha Calvarese, a labor leader and tireless fighter for working Coloradans, is the Democrat running against Lauren Boebert. In 2024 she had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district since we lost it in 2010 whereas Boebert underperformed Trump in every county.
[This seat is an easy pick up opportunity for Dems and we need your support . Will you please make a monthly contribution to help Trisha defeat Lauren Boebert and ensure we stay on track to take back the House?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ylo8_w4qHysT3UDvjae5JdcmP6U0LhfU8qWpb_nTsOfq8YR_VExNdIasJPPmIkh74ygJLln-89qmpb7egp5f8VOfa9PiNnjZAanWFuvSnJwVCVngIFCUtmYJ-viBvNeQPVNRLKARj9JH-nei8HRO_PkkSJMKXXaqqe8tgCPAbavJXZVD2_lE4N8eoaQsXXnx4w0GF-80Sorr0rHyRYUK37QATRW_vPNHtVoNRl33wmgLMrqEODYX4w==&c=GaFXefLVX8qdv_idC56BtK70BqEuvBN4dcQ1Erfr3aksATPsRak7dw==&ch=VA0JMYDQk-hYfa2qmyv54Zsk8OWulVvAOEzIQVqFsAKcwMmLmc0m6Q==)

clean_body: For Democrats to win in 2026, we need to counteract the seats we are losing to MAGA’s gerrymandering by going on the offensive in vulnerable districts like Lauren Boebert. | | | | | == | Peter, Lauren Boebert is spearheading MAGA's efforts to strip millions of their ability to vote ahead of the upcoming '26 midterm elections. Earlier this year, she wrote and introduced the SAVE ACT--a bill that she has tried to spin as "election security" but in reality is carte blanche for historic levels of voter suppression. This bill would make it harder for deployed service members, veterans, rural voters, and even women who took their spouse's last name (like Lauren Boebert) to cast a ballot. | == Boebert's bill is a foundational component of MAGA's strategy to suppress Democrat votes and redraw congressional maps in a desperate attempt to rig the outcome of the 2026 midterms. For Democrats to win in 2026, we need to counteract the seats we are losing to MAGA's gerrymandering by going on the offensive in vulnerable districts like Lauren Boebert's. We have seen record-breaking swings towards Democrats in red-leaning districts in elections across the country this year. CO-4 is only a R+9 district, well within the 10-15 point Democratic swings we have witnessed in elections throughout 2025. Trisha Calvarese, a labor leader and tireless fighter for working Coloradans, is the Democrat running against Lauren Boebert. In 2024 she had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district since we lost it in 2010 whereas Boebert underperformed Trump in every county. == Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | Thank you for your support, Team Trisha | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | | | | | | |

name: Leslie Mehta
subject: Rising costs, stagnant wages—Virginia deserves better.
disclaimer_text:
Paid For By
Leslie Mehta for Delegate
body:
Hi Peter,

I’ve knocked on thousands of doors in the last few months, and talked with so many of our lovely neighbors, and one of the biggest concerns I hear is about the cost of living. Inflation is squeezing budgets, tariffs are raising the price of everything, childcare is out of reach, and wages just can’t keep up.

It’s clear that what passes as “economic growth” in this country hasn’t touched most people’s lives.

We don’t need more trickle-down schemes that shower benefits on the already wealthy. What we need is an economy built for people who work, dream, and struggle just to make ends meet.

Here’s how I propose we get there:
* Raise wages so full-time work can support a family. No one working 40 hours a week should live in poverty.
* Crack down on wage theft and enforce labor protections. Workers should never fear retaliation for demanding fairness.
* Reform taxes so the burden is shared more equitably. It’s not fair for everyday families to carry the load while the richest pay less.
* Invest in people and communities. Quality schools, childcare, and skills training shouldn’t be privileges—they should be pathways.
* Hold corporations accountable. No more undue influence, no more bailouts, and no more cozy deals at the expense of your pocketbook.

When we win this race, I will work in Richmond every day to make these bold changes real. But to get there, we need your help now: because winning means not just having good values, but having the power to implement them.

If you believe we deserve an economy that works for everyone—not just the wealthy—please consider supporting us. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tfcwhyleslie?refcode2=txt1&refcode=20251022econ_b] Your contribution helps us make this fight louder and stronger.

Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tfcwhyleslie?refcode2=btndonate&refcode=20251022econ_b]
Get Involved [https://lesliemehta.com/get-involved/]

Thank you for believing in a fairer Virginia. The future we fight for is one where hard work is rewarded, not punished.

With determination,
Leslie

Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tfcwhyleslie?refcode2=footerbtn&refcode=20251022econ_b]
Volunteer [https://lesliemehta.com/get-involved/]
https://www.facebook.com/leslie4delegate/ [https://www.facebook.com/leslie4delegate/]
https://www.instagram.com/leslie4delegate [https://www.instagram.com/leslie4delegate]
https://twitter.com/lesliemehtava [https://twitter.com/lesliemehtava]

Paid For By
Leslie Mehta for Delegate
PO Box 1721
Midlothian, VA 23113
United States

name: Andy Ngo
subject: Help me bring Antifa to justice
body:
I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs

| |
---
| |
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zotg2vvklr1g5ddj8d5k6k20vvt2h9l0lljq66u8_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs)
---
My fellow concerned American, I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs.And unless we do something to stop them today, I won’t be the last. **[I’m asking you to donate $35 or more today to help me bring Antifa and its members who assaulted me to justice.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zi9vbkf1nvon1t1nct7smejd0mj60thr7av86dh8_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs) ****I was at Antifa's "ground zero" in Portland, Oregon during the summer of 2019.** This was before most Americans had ever heard of the Antifa movement, but they'd caused havoc and violence before.I went to an Antifa "protest" as a journalist to document and report on their “protest.”As soon as I arrived, I feared for my safety.The mob was angry, and violence always follows Antifa. Most of them wore masks to prevent being identified when they commit crimes. Many had on helmets and carried riot gear. Some, I later learned, secretly had weapons like baseball bats, brass knuckles and even bricks.I was scared, but I had a job to do, so I pressed on.**People like you needed to know what was happening.** But I didn't go unnoticed for long.Somebody recognized me and bashed me on the back of my head in an ambush."F*** you, Andy Ngo!" shouted voices around me. "Go home, fascist!"**Fists started flying at my face and head. Then came the projectiles.** Before I knew it, I was being repeatedly punched, kicked, and hit with objects.I tried to protect my head. I did not strike back as I knew that would only escalate the situation and put me in more danger.The thugs robbed me of my camera so that I wouldn’t have evidence of the assault. What they did to me is unacceptable and criminal. And now they're trying to do the same to other innocent Americans like you.That's why I sued members of the Rose City Antifa organization to bring them to justice and send the loud clear message that they are NOT above the law.Now I need your help to hold their feet to the fire and make sure justice is served. [**Will you donate $35 or more to help me hold Antifa accountable?**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zmsu66c6nrdph990a8379pvinn0pej23t02qu8t0_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs) This is bigger than me alone. After I got away from the mob, police finally showed up when I was in the hospital. But my attackers stole my evidence and nearly silenced me permanently with a traumatic brain injury.Later, cell phone footage from a bystander surfaced online. Thank goodness! The video captured part of the attack, clear as day.I hoped I'd get the justice I deserved.Wrong.To this day, six years later, the Portland Police have done nothing.**No charges. No arrests. No suspects questioned.** It's not much of a surprise, though.Radical politicians have long since bowed to the dangerous left-wing mobs that terrorize their streets. That’s why the Center for American Liberty helped me file a lawsuit against my attackers. A judge ordered three of them to pay damages – but more than a year later and they have not paid a dime.So now the Center for American Liberty is helping me track them down and make them pay for nearly killing me and trampling upon the First Amendment.Unfortunately it’s going to cost at least $20,000 to get justice.[**That's why I hope you'll join the fight by donating $35 or more to fund my efforts to hold Antifa and its members accountable.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zu2gqshj31dtt7i41vrnt1m9o7t4d50sh1d0egko_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs) We have to stop Antifa and make them face real, legal consequences for their destructive actions before they get to you and your family.Antifa wants the literal end of America -- and they'll stop at nothing to get it. Believe me when I say that's not an overstatement. Some members are avowed socialists or communists. But many are much worse – open violent anarchists who want to tear down civilization and remake it in their own twisted vision with no law and order.By any means necessary.**_Rioting. Destruction. Mayhem._** And they know, as long as they choose politically favored protests to co-opt, the media and politicians will never have the guts to denounce their violence or hold them accountable. They did that with BLM. They’re doing it now against ICE.It's a sick strategy, but very smart – because so far, they've been successful.**For the most part, they've gotten off scot-free.** Our politicians and media are treating this terrorist group like peaceful protestors!As we saw in Los Angeles earlier this summer, their riots will only spread and intensify if Antifa continue to escape consequences.**So now I'm taking justice into my own hands – with the help of the talented lawyers at the Center for American Liberty.**

[**You can be a part of bringing Antifa to justice with a donation of $35 or more today.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zl5327177v6pf89n7nsstlh1djvqlbuc97hp0ccg_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs) Though I'm seeking justice for myself, the impact of this case could be so much larger.This is about standing up for our country when our own leaders won't.This is about saving America as we know it and keeping you and your family safe.And this is just the first blow we intend to deliver.By taking them to court for the attack against me we can prove they are NOT untouchable. **[Can I count on you to donate $35 or more to help me get justice from Antifa?](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z9bm2j5mj22r0o80ja7rjdivm7qr9he98f1n2amg_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs) **Please don't wait – if we don't fight back and win now, **these violent thugs will be in EVERY city across America** , destroying everything and everyone in their path. Thank you in advance for your support.
---
| | Sincerely,
---
| | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zcnvs1m1am9s40200vrf2fi8h0mqlppgcgpkq9lo_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs)
---
**Andy Ngo**
---
|
---
| | P.S. I know firsthand that America's troubles with Antifa aren't over -- **they're just beginning**. Just look at what happened in Los Angeles earlier this summer. Today, we can make them face the consequences that left-wing politicians refuse to dole out. [**Please donate as generously as you can right away.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2ze00vdu8u99u1fnvi1d5ediffaeh8deoslh57v6g_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs)
---
[ **DONATE** ](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z1tnq9gfanu43155etfjobk6ua44la9piu66mpig_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs)
---
| | |
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z2k86mab5lfgpm3affgbe9fiu6m6f0ua0no1nm6g_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgecoj4cjh2bs)

**[She wasn't expecting him to do this...](https://substack.com/redirect/1bdcc595-2df8-4b81-9e93-7a04768a5959?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

* * *
# **Pissed-off reporter DESTROYS Chicago Mayor in front of the entire nation**
Chicago reporter William J. Kelly **[confronted Democrat Mayor Brandon Johnson](https://substack.com/redirect/adb156d8-fca1-4892-b7a7-ac3862b4d81f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during an intense press conference for demonizing law enforcement.
"You're the one having panic attacks!" Kelly said. "You're not keeping Chicago safe. What do you say to Donald Trump? How did you feel when Donald Trump called you incompetent?"
[](https://substack.com/redirect/adb156d8-fca1-4892-b7a7-ac3862b4d81f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/adb156d8-fca1-4892-b7a7-ac3862b4d81f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
**[Together with Natural Health Response](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# [RFK Jr to be fired?](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear good American,
On October 7, six former US Surgeon Generals published a letter trying to get **[RFK Jr.](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** fired.
But I noticed something very interesting...
Most of them have direct ties to the $1.7 TRILLION pharmaceutical industry.
_David Satcher served on the board of directors for Johnson & Johnson...
Jerome Adams serves on the board of directors for Atea Pharmaceuticals...
Richard Carmona served on the board of directors for McKesson...
Vivek Murthy founded a company to bring new drugs to market faster..._

It makes you wonder... What are they all so darn worried about?

Is it because **[you might find out about THIS?](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
P.S. Before the Deep State's smear campaign works, **[see RFK Jr's warning [HERE] of a new hidden epidemic.](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ba0a84-f09a-4152-b357-bdd95dce50db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* * *
# **Trump vows permanent cuts for "Democrat welfare programs" amid shutdown**
President Donald Trump told "Sunday Morning Futures" that the government shutdown, now nearing its third week, gives him authority to **[permanently eliminate what he called Democratic"welfare programs"](https://substack.com/redirect/37600594-1796-4c1a-9e36-0cdbf42c130c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that Republicans have long wanted to cut, claiming shutdown rules allow him to freeze or suspend certain federal programs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/37600594-1796-4c1a-9e36-0cdbf42c130c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Trump 's vow:** "They didn't realize that that gives me the right to cut programs that Republicans never wanted -- you know, giveaways, welfare programs," Trump said. "And we're doing that. We're cutting them permanently."

* **Shutdown triggered by ACA subsidy dispute:** The shutdown began after Democrats refused to approve a funding bill without an extension of enhanced Affordable Care Act subsidies set to expire in 2025, putting pay for hundreds of thousands of federal employees on hold, while the White House claims Democrats are pushing billions for Medicaid coverage for illegal immigrants.
* **Trump attacks Democrats ' political future:** The president characterized Democrats as "kamikaze pilots" with "no future," arguing they lack strong leadership and viable candidates, and that the shutdown stalemate gives Republicans leverage to cut billions in contested programs.

* * *
# "No Kings" protest produces massive crowds of Trump-hating Americans
Massive crowds participated in the second "No Kings" protest against the Trump administration on Saturday, with more than 100,000 people gathering across New York City's five boroughs and roughly 2,500 protests held nationwide, including in major cities and even London (where they really do have a king...).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/35ea5164-2106-43e0-ab62-ffbe2778a9fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **[Limited arrests in specific locations:](https://substack.com/redirect/f32670a0-01e2-46cb-9580-efe0663bace4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** A small number of arrests occurred in certain areas, including at least 15 near an ICE facility in Broadview, Illinois, three people detained in Portland after an alleged assault, and several arrests in Denver where police dispersed a group trying to access a highway. The demonstrations were largely peaceful, despite concerns about violence ahead of the marches.
* **Political divide over protests:** Republicans, including House Speaker Mike Johnson, argued the protests were efforts to distract from the government shutdown and appease the Democratic base, while Democratic leaders like California Gov. Gavin Newsom and Sen. Bernie Sanders defended the rallies as peaceful pushback against what they characterized as Trump's authoritarian policies.

## We were there! Footage coming soon...
PolitiBrawl was at the massive Washington D.C. march all day, footage from the day will be released on our Monday Evening Newsletter tonight. Conversations got pretty heated as soon as they figured out who we were.
**Tune in later to see what happens when a conservative tries to start a conversation in a crowd full of liberals ...**
* * *
# **Survivors outraged as Mamdani cozies up with imam linked to deadly terror attack**
NYC mayoral frontrunner Zohran Mamdani **[appeared Friday with Imam Siraj Wahhaj](https://substack.com/redirect/b14af918-3d26-4bcb-9c11-4967c84a140d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , who prosecutors named as a potential co-conspirator in the 1993 World Trade Center bombing trial that killed six people and injured over 1,000, calling him a "pillar" of the Brooklyn community.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/99462244-fcdb-48a6-b59b-8fd7377c0d7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Wahhaj 's extensive terrorism connections:** The imam served as a character witness for Omar Abdel-Rahman, a cleric later sentenced to life for orchestrating plans to murder hundreds of Americans, and three of Wahhaj's children were sentenced to life in 2024 for organizing a jihadist training compound in New Mexico used to plot attacks against the U.S. government.
* **Survivors condemn Mamdani 's meeting with Wahhaj:** Survivors of the 1993 bombing criticized NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani for posting a smiling photo with 75-year-old Imam Siraj Wahhaj, whom federal prosecutors considered an "indicted co-conspirator" in the attack that killed six people and injured over 1,000.
"These incidents are very personal to people in New York. They are our family members, our friends, our neighbors, people we care about," said Maria Danzilo, 69, whose sister was in the WTC the day of the '93 attack. "Why trigger people like this? It seems unnecessary. Don't you want to heal the past? Do we really want to make people so upset? It just seems like it's not good for the city after so many terrible things have happened."
* **Imam previously called for Islamic state:** Wahhaj once declared in a sermon that "this so-called democracy will crumble, and there will be nothing. And the only thing that will remain will be Islam," and several people connected to the World Trade Center bombing attended or visited his Brooklyn mosque around the time of the attack.
* **Financial support for Mamdani 's campaign:** Wahhaj posted a video endorsing Mamdani as a "very good candidate" and contributed $1,000 to a super PAC that subsequently funneled most of its money to a PAC solely backing Mamdani's mayoral bid, who currently leads the race with 52% support among likely voters.

clean_body: Friends, Let’s call it what it is. The Democratic Party has sold out to the radicals and communists. Help me fight back. | Friends, Let’s call it what it is. The Democratic Party has sold out to the radicals and communists. | The so-called “No Kings March” isn’t about justice or progress. The Communist Party, the Freedom Socialist Party, and the Democratic Socialists of America are taking over. | These are extremist groups that want to tear down everything that makes our country great. They want to defund our police, open our borders, and dismantle capitalism. And the Democratic Party is marching right alongside them. Friends, this is no longer the party of working-class Americans. | It’s the party of Marxists and anarchists. And they’re trying to take over our city and our country. As the only Republican member of Congress from New York City, I’m the last line of defense against their socialist takeover. I’m standing up to the mob, but I need your help. | | | | | | | | | | | | | Thank you for standing with me, Nicole | Nicole for New York, PO Box 60487, Staten Island, NY 10306

name: Eileen Laubacher
subject: let me introduce myself
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15]Hello,

My name is Eileen Laubacher, and I am the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral running against Lauren Boebert here in Colorado.

I was 17-years-old the first time I raised my right hand and took an oath to support and defend the Constitution against all enemies, foreign and domestic.

I took those words, and that responsibility, seriously every day for the next 4 decades. When I was an intelligence officer. When I was deployed to Afghanistan. When I became an Admiral. And as a member of the president’s National Security Council.

But in all those years, I have never felt as much of a threat to our country, our Constitution, and our very democracy as I do right now. I said I would never run for office. But in this moment, I feel an obligation to step up, serve my community, and defend our country once again.

I am running for Congress to defeat Lauren Boebert because when she was sworn into office, Lauren took a similar oath to defend our Constitution – and she has betrayed it almost every single day since then.

Adam Frisch only lost to Lauren Boebert by 546 votes. It was the closest race in the entire country. With the right Democrat, we can finally put an end to Lauren Boebert’s embarrassing political career – but I need your help now to do it.

My campaign has set a goal to raise $50,000 to defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House for Democrats, but right now, we’re still very short. If I am going to send Lauren Boebert packing and flip this seat blue, I need grassroots donors like you to chip in right now to my campaign:

Please, will you split a donation right now between my campaign and John Hickenlooper to help me defeat Lauren Boebert, flip this seat blue, and take back the House for Democrats? I am not accepting money from corporate PACs like Boebert is, so no amount you can give is too small. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15]

DONATE → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15]

Our republic is being tested in ways that it has never been before. We must protect it and keep it.

I am so grateful to have your support.

Eileen

Rear Admiral Eileen Laubacher (Retired)
United States Navy

REAR ADMIRAL EILEEN LAUBACHER (RET) IS A FORMER MEMBER OF THE U.S. NAVY. USE OF HER MILITARY RANK, JOB TITLES, AND PHOTOGRAPHS IN UNIFORM DOES NOT IMPLY ENDORSEMENT FROM THE DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY OR THE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE.
Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add hello@hickenlooper.com to your address book.
Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to unsubscribe: https://action.hickenlooper.com/p/cTrdfv93Ski9KUzCx_WudA2?unsubscribedata=ZWKFWt3yu2Rtd3sQWFv76ZkcfvG73ge62HVfLQ1HqFbskZhxLgoZekA2dvpm2glapl9OTdovICeVOkQ3su%2FyRd1tx5uprI0nltyvJMeBUew1CyB3smO6apznoZl6Wz4542CtnWZa2KID3tOOFVPYBOEqfNmIjbS0oOgbKSyZ%2FlGJMYBBrdzy20hAvQfZ4G4tNGc15rBjjsedrvQ7CpLDjVVtzhUQDC%2B2rSkt0Q8%2B6YExQ8VagLRrIZKNNacTJQSJTH%2BRBAyE1QmohofZnqwy7Zy%2FKQnGAZxbh%2FIPNk%2B9uGz12i066oFhn5EE6n7ALUBAIsF269nmwprqfJ2zUOeDM4qew6eP7HEd2varNfS6Ukb5mC01aHTJllu%2Bonfu9rwOC5FC3AMOMMjqmMjyu7VhND5eYMmqEgunFEWSujZFMrY%3D .

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: Cook Political Report says our race is a toss up.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Literally just a couple hundred votes could make the difference.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=862%2E181023%2EeGVZdT)
Team,
Before I ask you to donate to my campaign for Governor ahead of our upcoming fundraising deadline, please give me a chance to explain why your support and donation matter so much, especially in such a close race.
I hope you’ll keep reading, but if you are ready to make a donation now, here is a link to give: [secure.actblue.com/donate/jmb-em-q42025?refcode=FOR-YOU](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/2140?refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251020_AM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=862%2E181023%2EeGVZdT)
_Cook Political Report_ labeled Michigan one of five toss-up governors' races next year.
I’m not surprised – **races are won and lost here by the smallest of margins.** Literally just a couple hundred votes could make the difference.
But I’m not worried. This is my fourth statewide campaign, and I won my most recent statewide race in 2022 by 14 points. I know firsthand that we win through hard work, showing up in every community, listening to voters, and getting things done.
That’s what I’ve done as secretary of state. And that’s exactly what I’ll do as Michigan’s next governor.
**Even after winning races before, though, I still have people who doubt that Michigan can become the first state in the country to elect back-to-back female governors. So I need YOUR HELP to show the strength of our people-driven campaign.**
[That means hitting our fundraising goals, and we have a big one _tonight_. name: Andy Ngo
subject: Help me bring Antifa to justice
body:
I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs

| |
---
| |
---
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zvnt6d0748pace80retikbkggovp8hgra2h5du08_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs)
---
My fellow concerned American, I was one of the first savagely beaten by lawless, violent Antifa thugs.And unless we do something to stop them today, I won’t be the last. **[I’m asking you to donate $35 or more today to help me bring Antifa and its members who assaulted me to justice.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zvd35t79liqbti5oipvmgb2q2d3ct1jgrecsrqvo_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs) ****I was at Antifa's "ground zero" in Portland, Oregon during the summer of 2019.** This was before most Americans had ever heard of the Antifa movement, but they'd caused havoc and violence before.I went to an Antifa "protest" as a journalist to document and report on their “protest.”As soon as I arrived, I feared for my safety.The mob was angry, and violence always follows Antifa. Most of them wore masks to prevent being identified when they commit crimes. Many had on helmets and carried riot gear. Some, I later learned, secretly had weapons like baseball bats, brass knuckles and even bricks.I was scared, but I had a job to do, so I pressed on.**People like you needed to know what was happening.** But I didn't go unnoticed for long.Somebody recognized me and bashed me on the back of my head in an ambush."F*** you, Andy Ngo!" shouted voices around me. "Go home, fascist!"**Fists started flying at my face and head. Then came the projectiles.** Before I knew it, I was being repeatedly punched, kicked, and hit with objects.I tried to protect my head. I did not strike back as I knew that would only escalate the situation and put me in more danger.The thugs robbed me of my camera so that I wouldn’t have evidence of the assault. What they did to me is unacceptable and criminal. And now they're trying to do the same to other innocent Americans like you.That's why I sued members of the Rose City Antifa organization to bring them to justice and send the loud clear message that they are NOT above the law.Now I need your help to hold their feet to the fire and make sure justice is served. [**Will you donate $35 or more to help me hold Antifa accountable?**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zrsscnjn3qt5bv85slk2s5an2lk1p1183vrn3h40_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs) This is bigger than me alone. After I got away from the mob, police finally showed up when I was in the hospital. But my attackers stole my evidence and nearly silenced me permanently with a traumatic brain injury.Later, cell phone footage from a bystander surfaced online. Thank goodness! The video captured part of the attack, clear as day.I hoped I'd get the justice I deserved.Wrong.To this day, six years later, the Portland Police have done nothing.**No charges. No arrests. No suspects questioned.** It's not much of a surprise, though.Radical politicians have long since bowed to the dangerous left-wing mobs that terrorize their streets. That’s why the Center for American Liberty helped me file a lawsuit against my attackers. A judge ordered three of them to pay damages – but more than a year later and they have not paid a dime.So now the Center for American Liberty is helping me track them down and make them pay for nearly killing me and trampling upon the First Amendment.Unfortunately it’s going to cost at least $20,000 to get justice.[**That's why I hope you'll join the fight by donating $35 or more to fund my efforts to hold Antifa and its members accountable.**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z8nmvk4fttf3oavjphk0gpb3rtk7djrvfktdgdr0_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs) We have to stop Antifa and make them face real, legal consequences for their destructive actions before they get to you and your family.Antifa wants the literal end of America -- and they'll stop at nothing to get it. Believe me when I say that's not an overstatement. Some members are avowed socialists or communists. But many are much worse – open violent anarchists who want to tear down civilization and remake it in their own twisted vision with no law and order.By any means necessary.**_Rioting. Destruction. Mayhem._** And they know, as long as they choose politically favored protests to co-opt, the media and politicians will never have the guts to denounce their violence or hold them accountable. They did that with BLM. They’re doing it now against ICE.It's a sick strategy, but very smart – because so far, they've been successful.**For the most part, they've gotten off scot-free.** Our politicians and media are treating this terrorist group like peaceful protestors!As we saw in Los Angeles earlier this summer, their riots will only spread and intensify if Antifa continue to escape consequences.**So now I'm taking justice into my own hands – with the help of the talented lawyers at the Center for American Liberty.**

[**You can be a part of bringing Antifa to justice with a donation of $35 or more today.**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zcdq27qporhu3o8jl51hiipd5dvdtfn5eq0u3hhg_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs) Though I'm seeking justice for myself, the impact of this case could be so much larger.This is about standing up for our country when our own leaders won't.This is about saving America as we know it and keeping you and your family safe.And this is just the first blow we intend to deliver.By taking them to court for the attack against me we can prove they are NOT untouchable. **[Can I count on you to donate $35 or more to help me get justice from Antifa?](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z2gfa7l5o1msrbhajn4r3jf0i8187s4k1gr9v6eg_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs) **Please don't wait – if we don't fight back and win now, **these violent thugs will be in EVERY city across America** , destroying everything and everyone in their path. Thank you in advance for your support.
---
| | Sincerely,
---
| | [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z2e4aug6olvsrjn8a9dl5g9hbhmrpn8kojrhqq7o_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs)
---
**Andy Ngo**
---
|
---
| | P.S. I know firsthand that America's troubles with Antifa aren't over -- **they're just beginning**. Just look at what happened in Los Angeles earlier this summer. Today, we can make them face the consequences that left-wing politicians refuse to dole out. [**Please donate as generously as you can right away.**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zhooq58sfkvfuee4cba62rot41v9pbonkvvf0a88_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs)
---
[ **DONATE** ](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zdu603g42ger6bjsjqrp7b428h4q0oiptqr78pig_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs)
---
| | |
---
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zeots15tsje358m4hojc9ffgam2q55lsrdn412b8_rp22sh2s8i6eor0cjgeao36c1h2bs)

body:
Donald Trump is scared of Mikie Sherrill. That’s why he’s doing everything he can to flip New Jersey red this upcoming election
Peter, Donald Trump and his administration are scared of Mikie Sherrill.
That’s why they’re doing everything they can to flip New Jersey red this upcoming election — **including releasing Mikie’s personal military records, including her Social Security number, to an ally of opponent’s campaign. And Jack’s campaign disseminated those records too — while falsely smearing Mikie and her family.**
**[We need to send a message that the Trump administration’s illegal weaponization of the federal government _will not win them this race._ Can you split a donation between our team and Heartland Patriots to show Donald Trump and Jack Ciattarelli that our team will not be intimidated into silence?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96517?refcode=em20251020-9599&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9599%2E614464%2ENa8YBs)**

This isn’t just about Mikie, Peter. If Jack Ciattarelli will do this to Mikie — a veteran and a sitting member of Congress — what would he do with New Jerseyans’ private information if he got access to it as our governor?
**We need to win this race so he doesn’t get the chance.**
**[Jack Ciattarelli has the might of the Trump administration behind him. But we’re betting _everything_ that this grassroots coalition of Americans is stronger. Please, can you split a donation today between Mikie’s campaign and Heartland Patriots to help Mikie fight back against the Trump administration and protect New Jersey from MAGA extremism?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96517?refcode=em20251020-9599&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9599%2E614464%2ENa8YBs)**

Democrats can flip them both and take back the majority — but GOP megadonors are already flooding the field. Chip in now to help Democrats win Ohio and Florida and flip the Senate for good >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-twosenateseats?refcode=102025_1]
We didn’t expect Senate races in Ohio and Florida this cycle - but thanks to JD Vance and Marco Rubio joining Trump’s administration, both seats are up for grabs early.
That’s two unexpected chances to flip red seats and take back the Senate majority. And it’s happening fast.
Republicans are scrambling to defend these surprise openings, and their donors are already flooding cash into both states. If Democrats don’t move now, we risk falling behind before the race even begins.

Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.

Bolingbrook Police Refuse to Arrest Attackers Who Beat Federal Agents (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/10/bolingbrook-police-refuse-to-arrest-attackers-who-beat-federal-agents.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=e000336b71-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/10/bolingbrook-police-refuse-to-arrest-attackers-who-beat-federal-agents.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=e000336b71-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
By Illinois Review

In a shocking display of law enforcement negligence, the Bolingbrook Police Department declined to arrest two individuals who physically assaulted federal immigration agents on Sunday morning — even after the victims reported being struck repeatedly in the head while attempting to make a lawful arrest. The incident, which occurred in broad daylight, raises serious questions about whether Bolingbrook Police are enforcing the law or protecting lawbreakers.
CLICK TO READ FULL ARTICLE (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/10/bolingbrook-police-refuse-to-arrest-attackers-who-beat-federal-agents.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=e000336b71-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
Norine Hammond Betrays Conservatives – Backed Pritzker’s Law Letting Non-Citizens Serve as Police Officers (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/10/norine-hammond-betrays-conservatives-backed-pritzkers-law-letting-non-citizens-serve-as-police-officers.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=e000336b71-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/10/norine-hammond-betrays-conservatives-backed-pritzkers-law-letting-non-citizens-serve-as-police-officers.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=e000336b71-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
By Illinois Review

Illinois State Representative Norine Hammond’s voting record is under renewed fire as grassroots conservatives shine a spotlight on a series of Democrat-aligned votes that have resurfaced amid a growing storm of political controversies.

Hammond, the Deputy Republican Leader in the Illinois House, has long represented a deep-red district in western Illinois – where more than 70 percent of voters supported Donald Trump. Yet her voting record tells a very different story: one increasingly in lockstep with Gov. JB Pritzker’s progressive agenda.

At the center of the latest backlash is Hammond’s 2023 vote to allow non-citizens to serve as police officers – a Democrat-backed measure that became law on January 1, 2024. House Bill 3751 permits green-card holders and certain non-citizens with federal work authorization to be sworn in as officers in Illinois.

body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/105bd725e19b/breaking-news-citizens-only-voting-amendment-submitted-to-board-of-canvassers-8371146?e=ecf60db114)

---
|
