| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fabb4943-5cf3-49bb-85d4-b4ce839f1bb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYnl0aGViYWxsb3QuY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNzY2NzM5MTEsInMiOjUxNTY1NjYsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjA5ODc0NDMsImV4cCI6MjA3NjU2MzQ0MywiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.BjNnzWjZJYtCNXrf76jb1um9x9TDDdK-dccX16Guh3M?)
Say what you want about James Carville, but he was right when he coined that phrase for Bill Clinton's first presidential campaign. It's a lesson Democrats keep forgetting.
When people vote, it almost always comes down to 4 or 5 priorities:
* Putting food on the table
* Paying for housing and keeping the lights on
* Staying healthy without medical debt
* Making sure their kids get a good education
* Getting to work on time

All of these are economic issues. Even the issues that _don 't appear economic on the surface_ are tied back to the economy.
**You want to talk about safety?** Without jobs and opportunity, schools fail, crime rises, and food deserts form.
**You want to talk about immigration?** Deporting workers shrinks the workforce, raises food and construction costs, and slashes tax revenue.
**You want to talk about the environment?** Dirty air and water drive up healthcare costs and lower productivity. Climate change disrupts food supply and spikes grocery prices. Transitioning to clean energy requires affordable EVs and reliable transit.
The list goes on. But it all comes back to one thing: the economy.
Don't mistake this for a "Third Way" revival. Quoting Carville doesn't mean politicking like him. This isn't about running to the middle and punching left. It's about getting back to our Democratic roots: fighting for everyday Americans against billionaires who profit from their struggles--without compromising our values.
If we return to campaigning on the basics--home, bills, food, health, schools, and work--we win.
## **Voters Vote with their Wallets.**
Voters don't always vote with their hearts or even their brains. They vote with their wallets and their pocketbooks. And election after election, the economy tops the list:
* **[2024 Presidential Election:](https://substack.com/redirect/d0414de9-9b14-4797-b235-e89f3fbd03ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.
* **[2022 Midterms:](https://substack.com/redirect/c2d79118-6fdb-4f13-b1f6-681968f8727e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.
* **[2020 Presidential Election:](https://substack.com/redirect/505a90e7-9a99-4afc-883f-0b5470432d54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.
* **[2018 Midterms:](https://substack.com/redirect/80165a4a-ff16-4dfd-81c7-ba6b1bb92162?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** healthcare was #1, but the economy came right after.
* **[2016 Presidential Election:](https://substack.com/redirect/c68fbe53-aefc-415d-ba69-5f90ab1495ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the economy was the most critical issue.

You get the general idea. Cycle after cycle, it's been right there in front of us.
And yet, after every loss, strategists and pundits scratch their heads and ask: _" Was it because of abortion? Was it because she was a woman? Was it because we didn't talk enough about trans kids in schools?"_
**F** ***ING NO. The voters have been telling us what matters every single time--it's the economy.
And here's the kicker: it's not like voters are against us on social issues. In fact, most Americans agree with Democrats on nearly all of them:
* [63% believe](https://substack.com/redirect/43d7c357-8514-4084-a443-4fecba5afff0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) abortion should be legal.
* [68% support ](https://substack.com/redirect/b107b92f-1a14-4c9d-8733-f1dcfc873eb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)marriage equality and LGBTQ rights.
* [58% favor](https://substack.com/redirect/2b954908-2b35-46f9-b8cd-b8ca47ae70d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) stricter gun laws.
* [61% say](https://substack.com/redirect/66cd5b07-aee9-4cb3-8d18-c42119eb8b45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) climate change is a serious problem.
* [68% approve ](https://substack.com/redirect/f64d1cd8-26b0-4db3-b497-0d9a407bcd14?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)of labor unions (the highest in 60 years).
* [81% believe](https://substack.com/redirect/c122010c-ca5e-430d-898f-f5aa9d5ee1d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the criminal justice system needs reform.

These aren't controversial positions. The real divide isn't on social issues--it's on whether people feel like they can afford groceries, rent, gas, or healthcare. That's where elections are won and lost.
When it comes to voters' issues, the economy is king. And the state of the economy--or more importantly, how people _perceive_ the state of the economy--will dictate how they vote, especially in presidential years.
This isn't new. History has been screaming it at us:
[In 2008](https://substack.com/redirect/ddf2c014-ca59-4fe2-ae7d-b0c15de8fe28?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), America was in the midst of the Great Recession. Voters blamed George Bush and Republicans, and they chose Barack Obama.
[In 2016,](https://substack.com/redirect/893f6b93-c9e2-42cf-86e6-3beec211d37a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) plenty of voters still felt left behind after eight years of Obama. They wanted change and rolled the dice on Donald Trump.
[In 2020](https://substack.com/redirect/7a955d0a-13d7-4f36-ac28-8863dc3bfb1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Trump's disastrous handling of the economy during COVID sank him, so voters turned back to Joe Biden.
[In 2024,](https://substack.com/redirect/e6f59c76-da80-413e-854f-8b25b4f8043c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) people were feeling the crunch from inflation and rising costs. Whether the numbers said things were improving didn't matter--public perception said otherwise. And Biden and Harris paid the price.
## **Social Justice and Equality, But Make It Different**
What also matters is how Democrats are _perceived_ when it comes to handling the economy. And unfortunately, cycle after cycle, Democrats get cast as the party that doesn't understand bread-and-butter economics.
In 2024, voters said Trump would handle the economy better than Kamala Harris--54% to 45%. We all know how that ended.
[A recent poll by the Searchlight Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/ce7a3907-e342-43e0-a52e-c57ab0aaad05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) asked voters in battleground states what issues leaders should focus on: affordability, healthcare, and jobs. The same poll asked what issues voters think Democrats are focused on: climate change, LGBTQ+ issues, and healthcare. [The New York Times echoed](https://substack.com/redirect/54bc4137-0fc0-40e3-9da2-62fe4029c881?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the same thing earlier this year. The result? Voters conclude Democrats don't share their priorities.
And that's on us. For years, Democrats' biggest rallying cry has been "saving democracy." Don't get me wrong--it's a noble cause. But when people can't afford groceries, when rent is eating half their paycheck, when medical debt is crushing them, talk of authoritarianism or "the orangeman in the White House" doesn't land. It's not that they don't care--it's that survival comes first.
Here's the truth: **economic justice is social justice.** Equity isn't just about identity--it's about making sure people can afford a home, have healthcare that won't bankrupt them, and send their kids to good schools. Standing up for unions and organizing for workers is fighting for equity. Raising wages and lowering costs is fighting for equity. Yet Democrats too often silo these struggles as separate lanes.
The irony is, we already have the moral high ground. We're the ones fighting for the working class. Republicans are very open about the fact that they legislate for billionaires and their donors. And yet, because we don't tell the story well enough, we still lose the "economy" perception war.
If Democrats want to save democracy, we have to connect it directly to the economy. _**Not just what we 're saving it from--but what we're saving it for.**_
That's the difference between being on defense and actually giving voters a reason to turn out. When we focus only on warning about authoritarianism, we sound like we're asking people to vote out of fear. When we connect democracy to lowering grocery bills, keeping people in their homes, protecting unions, and building an economy that works for everyone, we give them something to fight for.
**Because we need to give voters something to vote for, not just something to vote against.**
The closest Democrats have come to recentering the economy in recent years has been through the _Fighting Oligarchy_ tour with Senator Bernie Sanders and Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez. Together, they've gone into red and purple districts that too many Democrats write off, hammering on affordability, union power, and the corrosive influence of billionaires.
What's striking is not just their message--it's their audience. They've drawn large, diverse crowds in areas represented by Republicans who haven't lifted a finger for working families. Instead of talking past people, they've spoken to the pain of rising grocery bills, unaffordable housing, and medical debt. They've shown voters that _Democrats can be the party of the working class again,_ not just the party warning about Trump.
That's the blueprint: speak directly to material struggles, tie them to corporate greed and billionaire dominance, and show how progressive economic policy improves people's daily lives.
Because here's the bottom line: voters know Republicans are fighting for billionaires. If we don't prove--loudly and clearly--that Democrats are fighting for them, we'll keep losing ground. And instead of chasing the Right's talking points, we need to double down on the economic fights that actually win people's trust.
### **Don 't play down to their level.**
One flag I want to reiterate is this: in 2024, instead of focusing on the issues voters told us were the most important, too many Democrats decided to chase voters who were never on our side to begin with.
Representatives like **[Seth Moulton](https://substack.com/redirect/e6bcae1c-f876-445a-a681-8333ef21934c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and **[Tom Suozzi](https://substack.com/redirect/5e7eea8b-6c87-4708-9171-3e7ca0c3a2eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** were quick to scapegoat marginalized groups like trans kids in sports as the reason Democrats lost ground. Others went even further.
[Forty-six Democrats in the House](https://substack.com/redirect/9490c5a7-40bd-4272-8f72-f990c5a3b64c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [eleven in the Senate](https://substack.com/redirect/d7d38242-3d0c-4ea7-8401-a1f63c3e62f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) voted for the flawed **Laken Riley Act** , a bill that demonizes immigrants and trashes due process. That single vote helped pave the way for Trump's ICE-led mass deportations.
[Thirty-one House Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/40bf9350-54c7-4af5-8a59-6772e51325fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) even voted to give Trump and Republicans full control of sentencing laws in the District of Columbia--this after many had publicly argued that crime in DC wasn't nearly as bad as Fox News was making it out to be. Their votes weren't about solving problems; they were about fear and expediency.
These betrayals sent a clear message to the base: instead of fighting for you, we'll throw you under the bus if we think it'll win us a couple of "swing voters" who were never with us in the first place.
Let's be blunt: if the number one issue keeping someone from voting for Kamala Harris was **transgender athletes** , they were _never_ going to vote for us. And yet, we wasted precious oxygen and political capital pandering to these voters, while ignoring the ones who actually could have made the difference--the disaffected millions who sat out the election because they feel both parties have abandoned them and that both parties are the same.
And while I reject the premise, I understand the frustration.
That's the tragedy. Because every time Democrats echo right-wing frames or cave on issues of justice, we reinforce the very narrative that fuels the "both sides are the same" cynicism. And that cynicism is poison. It keeps working families, young people, and communities of color--our base--from showing up
That's why the campaigns that are cutting through are the ones centering kitchen table economics. Rep. Mikie Sherrill rolled out a ["Save You Time and Money"](https://substack.com/redirect/ae2ef4ec-0cf8-4e8a-a811-751e178c8898?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) agenda in her New Jersey gubernatorial bid, hammering affordability as her north star. State Senator Zohran Mamdani has put economic justice at the center of [his New York City Mayoral platform](https://substack.com/redirect/098a8539-7102-426f-b3f2-204058ef7fdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by emphasizing housing affordability, grocery prices, and childcare costs. And here in Virginia, Rep. Abigail Spanberger launched her ["Affordable Virginia"](https://substack.com/redirect/54ce8b6f-4da0-4a86-966a-18fd8fb3fdce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) plan for governor--[contrasting her agenda for lower costs](https://substack.com/redirect/a05a6def-1883-4ad9-9a00-a8657ce91eb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with Lt. Governor Winsome Sears' record of supporting Trump's Big Beautiful Bill, which raised costs for everyday Americans.
And how has Sears responded? With ads about...trans kids in schools and trans athletes.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/aa6db210-fb1a-46d1-b351-31dce30b673e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is the contrast. One side is offering voters real solutions to make their lives easier and more affordable. The other side is obsessing over culture war fights that have nothing to do with your rent, your grocery bill, or your paycheck.
The answer isn't to play down to their level. It's to raise the stakes back up--to show voters that Democrats stand for them, not against them.
## **The Blueprint For Us, Not Just Against Them**
I have said it before, and I will say it again: we need to give the voters a reason to vote for us and not just against them.
We don't have to reinvent the wheel. Democrats already have the blueprint for how to win, and it dates back nearly a century. Franklin Delano Roosevelt showed us exactly what happens when a party is bold enough to take on concentrated wealth and put working families first. His New Deal didn't just provide relief in the darkest days of the Depression -- it rebuilt faith in government, created millions of jobs, and gave people a reason to believe that politics could change their lives for the better.
Every time Democrats have remembered that lesson, we've built durable coalitions and made progress. When we've drifted away from it, we've lost ground. The War on Poverty, Medicare, Social Security, and the expansion of civil rights all grew from the idea that government should be on the side of ordinary people. But in the decades since, too many Democrats bought into austerity politics and Wall Street talking points, drifting toward centrist triangulation instead of doubling down on the bread-and-butter fights that actually win elections.
Republicans have no shame in telling us outright that their agenda is to serve the billionaire class. That's their side of the fight. Our side has to be clear and unapologetic about ours: to serve working people. Economic justice is social justice. Affordable housing, strong unions, good schools, and access to healthcare aren't just policies -- they're the building blocks of a fair society. And Democrats have the legacy, the record, and the moral high ground to make that case.
This isn't just a national strategy--it's already happening on the ground. In Virginia, Delegate Joshua Cole has made his campaign merch all about "affordable housing, good-paying jobs, excellent schools, & affordable prescriptions." [Delegate Michael Feggans has leaned ](https://substack.com/redirect/5ae1c939-d401-48d9-bb38-a108d56da4d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)into his veteran background, pointing out how Trump's cuts hurt Virginia's workforce, veterans' healthcare, and military families in his district. And Lily Franklin, running in one of the most competitive districts in the Commonwealth, pushed back on attacks about her working-class roots by embracing them--[showing in her ads](https://substack.com/redirect/275f57d3-3900-4344-ad03-4affff4f6177?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that she is just like so many voters she hopes to represent.
These aren't side battles; they're the fight. And if Democrats stick to them, we win.
Because at the end of the day, voters don't want culture war nonsense. They don't want scapegoats. They want to know how they're going to pay for groceries, keep the lights on, see a doctor, and give their kids a future.
Republicans are handing us the playbook when they rant about trans kids or demonize immigrants. They're telling us they've got nothing real to offer. That's our opening. We don't need to match them punch for punch on outrage--we need to hammer home that Democrats are the ones fighting for your paycheck, your health, your kids, and your home.
So here's the bottom line:**It 's the economy, damn it. It's always been the economy. And the party that remembers that will be the party that wins.**
**And the message is that no matter who you are, where you 're from, who you love, how you worship, what your family looks like: you deserve to get ahead and not just get by. Period. It's that simple.**
**And if the economy you are selling isn 't working for voters--like we tried to do in 2024--they won't buy it.**
### **TL;DR**
Democrats keep overthinking elections, but the truth has never changed: it's the economy, stupid. Voters consistently rank affordability, healthcare, and jobs as their top issues--yet Democrats too often focus on abstract ideas like "saving democracy" or get dragged into right-wing culture wars.
Cycle after cycle, public perception of who handles the economy better has swung elections--from Obama in 2008, to Trump in 2016, to Biden in 2020, and back to Trump in 2024. If people feel their wallets are hurting, they'll vote for change--no matter who's in charge.
Social issues still matter, but most Americans already side with Democrats on abortion rights, LGBTQ equality, unions, and climate action. What they want is a party that connects those fights back to their daily lives: lower grocery bills, affordable housing, decent healthcare, and good jobs.
When Democrats lead with pocketbook issues--as Bernie Sanders, AOC, Joshua Cole, Michael Feggans, Lily Franklin, and others have--voters respond. When they chase right-wing frames or scapegoat marginalized groups, they lose.
The blueprint exists: FDR's New Deal, working-class populism, and campaigns rooted in economic justice. To win, Democrats don't just need to run _against_ Trump--they need to give people something to run _for_.
**Bottom line: it 's still the economy, damn it--and whoever remembers that first will win.**

Republicans have no shame in telling us outright that their agenda is to serve the billionaire class. That's their side of the fight. Our side has to be clear and unapologetic about ours: to serve working people. Economic justice is social justice. Affordable housing, strong unions, good schools, and access to healthcare aren't just policies -- they're the building blocks of a fair society. And Democrats have the legacy, the record, and the moral high ground to make that case. This isn't just a national strategy--it's already happening on the ground. In Virginia, Delegate Joshua Cole has made his campaign merch all about "affordable housing, good-paying jobs, excellent schools, & affordable prescriptions." his veteran background, pointing out how Trump's cuts hurt Virginia's workforce, veterans' healthcare, and military families in his district. And Lily Franklin, running in one of the most competitive districts in the Commonwealth, pushed back on attacks about her working-class roots by embracing them-- that she is just like so many voters she hopes to represent. These aren't side battles; they're the fight. And if Democrats stick to them, we win. Because at the end of the day, voters don't want culture war nonsense. They don't want scapegoats. They want to know how they're going to pay for groceries, keep the lights on, see a doctor, and give their kids a future. Republicans are handing us the playbook when they rant about trans kids or demonize immigrants. They're telling us they've got nothing real to offer. That's our opening. We don't need to match them punch for punch on outrage--we need to hammer home that Democrats are the ones fighting for your paycheck, your health, your kids, and your home. So here's the bottom line:It 's the economy, damn it. It's always been the economy. And the party that remembers that will be the party that wins. And the message is that no matter who you are, where you 're from, who you love, how you worship, what your family looks like: you deserve to get ahead and not just get by. Period. It's that simple. And if the economy you are selling isn 't working for voters--like we tried to do in 2024--they won't buy it. TL;DR Democrats keep overthinking elections, but the truth has never changed: it's the economy, stupid. Voters consistently rank affordability, healthcare, and jobs as their top issues--yet Democrats too often focus on abstract ideas like "saving democracy" or get dragged into right-wing culture wars. Cycle after cycle, public perception of who handles the economy better has swung elections--from Obama in 2008, to Trump in 2016, to Biden in 2020, and back to Trump in 2024. If people feel their wallets are hurting, they'll vote for change--no matter who's in charge. Social issues still matter, but most Americans already side with Democrats on abortion rights, LGBTQ equality, unions, and climate action. What they want is a party that connects those fights back to their daily lives: lower grocery bills, affordable housing, decent healthcare, and good jobs. When Democrats lead with pocketbook issues--as Bernie Sanders, AOC, Joshua Cole, Michael Feggans, Lily Franklin, and others have--voters respond. When they chase right-wing frames or scapegoat marginalized groups, they lose. The blueprint exists: FDR's New Deal, working-class populism, and campaigns rooted in economic justice. To win, Democrats don't just need to run against Trump--they need to give people something to run for. Bottom line: it 's still the economy, damn it--and whoever remembers that first will win. Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? What is By the Ballot?** By the Ballot is a free SubStack that features unapologetic dispatches from the New South 's next generation of Democrats. Commentary on stances, tactics, and news on the Left. We don't claim to know everything or always be right, we just want to be a part of the conversation. While there 's many different ways to power and the Right wants to take over our country by hook or by crook, we want to help Democrats help Americans by making a government for the people by the ballot. Make sure to follow us on socials to stay up to date on our work! You can find By the Ballot on: Contact us at substackbytheballot@gmail.com | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Matt Royer Herndon, VA 20171 ? ?

Adam’s pursuit of justice and accountability is never-ending, Derek — especially during the Trump administration.
His latest fight? **_Protecting our First Amendment right to freedom of speech._**
In response to ABC suspending Jimmy Kimmel from the air for several days at the direction of the FCC, Adam is leading a Senate probe into the actions of the FCC Chairman that led to the silencing of a comedian and attacking a news network.
Adam knows that Trump is weaponizing the government departments against his political enemies and critics of his administration. It’s undemocratic, and it’s BS.
As a top target of Trump himself, Adam knows how important it is to take on the fight for our freedoms of speech. We’re a democracy, not an autocracy, try as Trump might to change that. And we need fighters like Adam speaking out.

name: Justice Democrats
subject: The Tennessee Three
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

You may know our most recent 2026 congressional endorsement, Justin J.
Pearson, as one of the Tennessee Three. If you haven’t heard about this or
forget the details, let us share the inspiring story with you.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251020.r3oEn-&refcode=em20251020-37123&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta ]But first, will you chip in any amount to help us send Justin to
represent TN-09 in Congress?

On March 27, 2023, a horrific mass shooting was carried out at the
Covenant School, an elementary school in Nashville, Tennessee, killing
three nine-year-old students and three staff members.

This violent tragedy was not an isolated incident, but part of the
worsening gun violence epidemic running rampant in our country, as leaders
in the pockets of the NRA and gun industry refuse — time after time — to
step in and pass commonsense gun reform to save lives.

Three days after this mass shooting, hundreds of community members
gathered at the Tennessee state capitol to make it clear: thoughts and
prayers are not enough — our children and communities deserve action.

Three Democratic lawmakers, including Rep. Justin J. Pearson, courageously
chose to join their constituents in peaceful protest, chanting “No action,
no peace" through a bullhorn on the House floor’s public chambers. From
that moment on, the Republican supermajority was determined to make an
example out of them, and they became known as the Tennessee Three.

In an unprecedented and authoritarian move, Tennessee Republicans made it
their mission to punish Justin and his colleagues for exercising their
First Amendment rights by voting for their expulsion, under the guise of
“breached decorum” — a move usually reserved for serious misconduct by
lawmakers.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251020.r3oEn-&refcode=em20251020-37123&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta ]Politico: What to know about ‘the Tennessee three’ after two Dems are
expelled. Reps. Justin Jones and Justin Pearson were both booted from the
state House, while the vote to expel Rep. Gloria Johnson failed. Photo of
Justin, Justin, and Gloria holding their hands in the air.

Rep. Gloria Johnson was spared expulsion by one vote, but Justin and his
fellow colleague, Rep. Justin Jones, were expelled from their seats. The
GOP cynically tried to silence two young, Black leaders for standing side
by side with their constituents and calling for action to save our
children from the wrath of gun violence.

As Justin wrote in a CNN opinion piece, “In Tennessee, the birthplace of
the Ku Klux Klan and where multiple Klan, White Nationalist, and neo-Nazi
organizations still thrive, the condescension and barely-veiled racism
heaped on Justin Jones and me, and on the communities we represent, were
on full display.”

What came next was a nationwide uproar. Justin did not give up or go
silent — he continued to raise his voice, speak truth to power, and rally
community members in the fight for justice.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251020.r3oEn-&refcode=em20251020-37123&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta ]Image from Bazaar photoshoot of Justin Jones and Justin Pearson

Six days after his wrongful expulsion, Justin was reappointed to his seat
by the Shelby County Board of Commissioners in a unanimous vote, and then
won a special election to retain his seat that August with almost 94% of
the vote.

It goes to show that true power is with the people, and far-right forces —
from the Tennessee Republican Party to Donald Trump and his MAGA cult in
Congress — know that and are afraid of it. That’s why they’re attacking
our most fundamental freedoms, but together, we can stop them.

Justin is ready to take this fighting spirit to Washington on behalf of
the working people of Memphis and join Justice Democrats like Summer Lee,
Rashida Tlaib, and Delia Ramirez in working to stop fascism while pushing
for bold solutions to gun violence, economic inequality, climate change,
and more.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251020.r3oEn-&refcode=em20251020-37123&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1005&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta ]But we’re going up against powerful, wealthy forces, so it’s going to
take ALL of us to win. Your contribution of $5 or more will
directly fuel our movement and help send him there: please pitch in
whatever you can.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251020.r3oEn-&refcode=em20251020-37123&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1024&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251020-37123-footer&t=1025&refcode2=37123_1203867_NCn9Ta&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1026&akid=37123%2E1203867%2ENCn9Ta

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Eileen Laubacher
subject: let me introduce myself -
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15]Hello,

My name is Eileen Laubacher, and I am the Democrat and retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral running against Lauren Boebert here in Colorado.

I was 17-years-old the first time I raised my right hand and took an oath to support and defend the Constitution against all enemies, foreign and domestic.

I took those words, and that responsibility, seriously every day for the next 4 decades. When I was an intelligence officer. When I was deployed to Afghanistan. When I became an Admiral. And as a member of the president’s National Security Council.

But in all those years, I have never felt as much of a threat to our country, our Constitution, and our very democracy as I do right now. I said I would never run for office. But in this moment, I feel an obligation to step up, serve my community, and defend our country once again.

I am running for Congress to defeat Lauren Boebert because when she was sworn into office, Lauren took a similar oath to defend our Constitution – and she has betrayed it almost every single day since then.

Adam Frisch only lost to Lauren Boebert by 546 votes. It was the closest race in the entire country. With the right Democrat, we can finally put an end to Lauren Boebert’s embarrassing political career – but I need your help now to do it.

My campaign has set a goal to raise $50,000 to defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House for Democrats, but right now, we’re still very short. If I am going to send Lauren Boebert packing and flip this seat blue, I need grassroots donors like you to chip in right now to my campaign:

Please, will you split a donation right now between my campaign and John Hickenlooper to help me defeat Lauren Boebert, flip this seat blue, and take back the House for Democrats? I am not accepting money from corporate PACs like Boebert is, so no amount you can give is too small. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15]

DONATE → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-hickenlooper-laubacher?amount=15]

Our republic is being tested in ways that it has never been before. We must protect it and keep it.

I am so grateful to have your support.

Eileen

Rear Admiral Eileen Laubacher (Retired)
United States Navy

REAR ADMIRAL EILEEN LAUBACHER (RET) IS A FORMER MEMBER OF THE U.S. NAVY. USE OF HER MILITARY RANK, JOB TITLES, AND PHOTOGRAPHS IN UNIFORM DOES NOT IMPLY ENDORSEMENT FROM THE DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY OR THE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE.

Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. name: TEST (via Senate Leadership Fund)
subject: Check your inbox, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Senate Leadership Fund

This whole year, my opponent has spent more time groveling for Trump's endorsement than standing up for Virginia.
In fact, Winsome admits that she met privately with Trump to plead for his support.
This came at a time when the President was firing thousands of federal workers, driving up prices, and planning to cut funding for healthcare and public schools — actions which have devastated Virginia's economy and threatened the livelihoods of so many.
It's clear that Winsome places her loyalty to Trump above everything else.
_Now, I'm using every tool at my disposal to make sure voters know the truth about Winsome._ But we're in the heart of early voting now, and sharing our message on TV, at the doors, and over the internet is so incredibly expensive.
_So, can I count on your support today, Peter?_
**[If you've been waiting for the moment when you can have the greatest impact on our campaign for Governor, this is it. clean_body: I need each of my supporters to join our donor list by midnight. = Peter, I'm going to lay this out as clearly as I can: Donald Trump just endorsed my opponent Winsome Earle-Sears late last night. I'm the only Democrat running for Governor this year in a state controlled by Republicans, and I need each of my supporters to join our donor list by midnight. It's urgent so that we can continue standing up to President Trump, turning out voters, and demonstrating the strength of this campaign. = If you are signed up for ActBlue Express, your contribution to Abigail Spanberger will be processed immediately: = = = = = = This whole year, my opponent has spent more time groveling for Trump's endorsement than standing up for Virginia. In fact, Winsome admits that she met privately with Trump to plead for his support. This came at a time when the President was firing thousands of federal workers, driving up prices, and planning to cut funding for healthcare and public schools — actions which have devastated Virginia's economy and threatened the livelihoods of so many. It's clear that Winsome places her loyalty to Trump above everything else. Now, I'm using every tool at my disposal to make sure voters know the truth about Winsome. But we're in the heart of early voting now, and sharing our message on TV, at the doors, and over the internet is so incredibly expensive. So, can I count on your support today, Peter? = = Thank you for standing with me. Let's win this together. Abigail Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. Message & data rates may apply. Text HELP for more info. Text STOP to stop receiving messages. . Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States

As Oregon’s Secretary of State and a member of the State Board of Education, I take our state’s K-12 education system extremely seriously. As parents and educators become increasingly aware of the impact of social media and cell phone usage on students, conversations about the potential need to ban cell phone use in schools have gained momentum. **That’s why I’m reaching out today. I wanted to get your input on this critical question for the future of education and the raising of our kids. Your input is very important to me.** [**So let me know: Do YOU support banning cell phones in public schools?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117654471/577190979/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDMwNGU2My1lZGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lyCSwKJKudQNqyFJ6fX3JXKBktSpLX6ypMmCeqLNox0=&emci=851ac450-c3ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=2d304e63-edad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=30662126)

[ **ANSWER NOW >>** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117654475/577190982/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDMwNGU2My1lZGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lyCSwKJKudQNqyFJ6fX3JXKBktSpLX6ypMmCeqLNox0=&emci=851ac450-c3ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=2d304e63-edad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=30662126)
---
The work to build a world-class education system for Oregon’s kids is never done. There will always be more for us to do when it comes to building a public school system Oregon can be proud of — and I’m ready to dig in. **However, with such divisive questions facing our state over the next few years, I wanted to solicit your input on some of the most pressing issues.** [**So please, spare just a few seconds to let me know where you stand on the issue of cell phones in the classroom. Do you support banning them from our schools?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117654477/577190985/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZDMwNGU2My1lZGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lyCSwKJKudQNqyFJ6fX3JXKBktSpLX6ypMmCeqLNox0=&emci=851ac450-c3ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=2d304e63-edad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=30662126)
---
Thank you, Tobias Read
Oregon Secretary of State

clean_body: I had to keep this short because I don’t have a lot of time. | Fellow American, We’re already more than halfway through October and I’m already in serious trouble. My campaign fell $1,020 short of its mid-month fundraising goal. If I fail to get our fundraising back on track by the end of the month, we’ll signal to Democrats that we don’t have the necessary grassroots support to defend this Republican seat. For America, Steve Daines Sent from my iPhone Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

Team —
Give us a moment to explain why Senator Sherrod Brown deserves our attention.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117654634/577191579/-736798564?refcode=AU_SMP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC30_202501020_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODY3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDMyMzZhNS1lZWFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yq6LVHbp4bfE8xpm3COGXsX0ci9YqXX3wCSnGg0l36s=)
**Sherrod Brown was born and raised in Ohio. He’s dedicated his life to fighting for Ohioans and the Dignity of Work.** He believes that hard work should pay off for everyone — no matter who you are, where you live, or what kind of work you do.
Years ago, a union steelworker gave him a pin of a yellow canary in a birdcage. It’s a reminder of a time when miners would bring canaries into the mines to check for dangerous levels of gas.
If the canary died, the miners knew to get out. At that time, workers didn’t have strong unions or a government that would fight for them.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117654635/577191580/-736798564?refcode=AU_SMP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC30_202501020_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODY3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDMyMzZhNS1lZWFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yq6LVHbp4bfE8xpm3COGXsX0ci9YqXX3wCSnGg0l36s=)
Sherrod has worn this canary pin ever since to remember the workers he’s fighting so hard for every single day. It’s a reminder that the fight is still going — to support our country and the people who make it work.
Sherrod has taken on the tough fights in the U.S. Senate — working to stand up for working men and women against big corporations.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117654636/577191581/-736798564?refcode=AU_SMP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC30_202501020_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODY3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZDMyMzZhNS1lZWFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yq6LVHbp4bfE8xpm3COGXsX0ci9YqXX3wCSnGg0l36s=)
**But now, Republicans are preparing to pour MILLIONS into attacking Sherrod and keeping his seat red.**

| | Fellow American, I don't say this lightly, but it's God's honest truth... Right now, the Democrats are the party of anger and rage. They hate President Trump, plain and simple. **And make no mistake, that will translate at the ballot box.** _Yes, President Trump and Republicans are delivering victory after victory ..._ _Yes, we are winning on truly every key issue with voters ..._ _Yes, we are more popular than the Democrats ..._ **But at the end of the day, if the anger and hatred among the Left translates to voter enthusiasm and turnout**** _(_**_**_and it already is!_**_**_)_****, then nothing else will matter.** Sadly, this **"enthusiasm gap** " among our voters and the Democrats is the uphill battle we're facing for the midterms. And it's not looking good, Fellow American. Special election after special election, Republicans are getting TROUNCED this year in voter turnout! Now, the fate of President Trump's majority and his presidency rests on how we respond, how we engage our own voters ahead of the midterms. _That 's why I've recorded this special voicemail for trusted patriots like YOU to let you in on my plan to FIGHT BACK and DEFEND President Trump's majority_. **Please take just a moment to listen to the message I 've recorded for you. >> [[link]](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=c6d8196b157244e7ba8952c839b80d06&_e=yoWk8rKruTRSIqpoaO-8ccYFF50Ey3Xx74na8uXOFkRivzNpwUrkKtGRdQALtS0w0oLek1D-7AqR15owHX08z5lu8UeGmsMKke1l37r16pwLGDT9WMEoOOcxENp73SpRpFcjkqmnxfBQt6f5Ua2t2RQpVy_5lCkdEi3RnlEC3r1OXBZGo01kP4gAXp56U46V53VSY-ME468o9cS0ido0DGKgJ4pnjnLwiVMFOUBy_KRxuWaKMQM93SMBI4UKe5DbDYmp45nifAyWw2hvD3fuF0J-jNSU4ScoRL6818r0tLzzSoqPlV1Nt1OYJDAIsr1lmc38rcaA3O5ZVGJdBz5lBv95YHTZpWwUAKQu0KJl863UdCNyiBvA-B-6Txz6DXJBZWF2GtTWjybBtgghnFKpJH7jnMz1XBA1EZhbhjXfcaEGcsPn39o6SBIedrb2X7VZ747OC57v1feTnPADT_hQM134S0WyTbVx1FYI_GUAfCpI8sFo5-2DZObifM9dP6Edo1yEp9z2cqdcF40eLTpPb-1QCXf5mJlIFB8qbew6_qwdX62gkg4VWAwSh77rxwxIzRt_J5RWeCpnWmDMzVmYPHgNkupBjOM5zmnaLDbXp023-KxgCpoLmwkzLn5pKUUWoJMFfheuz05vaKR6Z9OzZCrfZxmYFqKijOhITCDJkzcQi8TGY1QkhR7prijr0e_-5WW1kOYpEbCte68bdIIZhNkvP_XfWHDRWLXLITagaKSvLk0cBV8Lj1xslYGPYj4_BlGm-IXULPV-_aFf754a0R250kIj8VHBK30qC0icPClbWHa5_g6fjdIyg2lx_cIukLncPXCZk-9nADdqHKQi6A%3D%3D)**

Yes, President Trump and Republicans are delivering victory after victory ... Yes, we are winning on truly every key issue with voters ... Yes, we are more popular than the Democrats ... But at the end of the day, if the anger and hatred among the Left translates to voter enthusiasm and turnout (and it already is!), then nothing else will matter. Sadly, this "enthusiasm gap " among our voters and the Democrats is the uphill battle we're facing for the midterms. And it's not looking good, Fellow American. Special election after special election, Republicans are getting TROUNCED this year in voter turnout! Now, the fate of President Trump's majority and his presidency rests on how we respond, how we engage our own voters ahead of the midterms. That 's why I've recorded this special voicemail for trusted patriots like YOU to let you in on my plan to FIGHT BACK and DEFEND President Trump's majority. Please take just a moment to listen to the message I 've recorded for you. >> [[link]]() [ [LISTEN NOW] ]() Thank you, Ted Cruz [ [LISTEN NOW] ]() | | | Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate | | PO Box 25400 Houston, TX 77265 | | | |

| 2025 GENERAL ELECTION EARLY VOTING ANALYSIS
---
| Early voting is officially underway across Georgia. Our mission at Greater Georgia is to educate and mobilize voters -- and the initial results show there is more work to be done!
As of October 19, statewide turnout is at 1.31%. The top performing counties (as a percent of the early voting electorate) are:
Fulton (19.4%) DeKalb (8.2%) Cobb (5.8%) Gwinnett (6.2%) Fayette (2.9%) Henry (2.9%) Houston (2.6%) Clayton (2.5%) Chatham (2.4%) Coweta (1.8%)
Our early voting analysis shows that turnout varies widely by county, and too many voters still don't realize there's a statewide election on the ballot this fall. That's why our mission, to educate, engage, and mobilize Georgia voters, matters more than ever.
Use this tool to see how your county is performing so far in early voting. Use it as motivation to reach out to your friends and neighbors. Remind them to vote before Halloween, or on Election Day!
If you want to join our team canvassing and phone banking, email Dylan Blitz at dylan@greatergeorgia.com to join our efforts. Every conversation makes a difference!

| | Dear Fellow Conservative,

Enough is enough. I'm glad to see the Trump administration is taking action to combat the rising tide of left-wing violence in America.

After years of witnessing Antifa members incite and commit violence against innocent people, attack law enforcement officials, and torch federal and private property, President Trump officially designated Antifa as a domestic terrorist organization.

This is a move The Heritage Foundation's scholars have promoted since 2019. It will prioritize attention and federal resources on Antifa and provide tools for stopping the funding that allows Antifa to operate.

Antifa is not just "a set of ideas." It is an organization with a logo, trained activists, funding, and uses pre-made signs and pre-gathered vehicles, gear, and projectiles.

This is a great first step. But there is even more President Trump can do to protect Americans from leftist violence.

The Heritage Foundation is urging the FBI to designate Transgender Ideology-Inspired Violent Extremism (TIVE) as domestic terrorism, following a string of attacks by trans or trans-supporting criminals.

Especially in the aftermath of the Charlie Kirk assassination, this action is long overdue.

clean_body: Enough is enough. | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | | | | | | Dear Fellow Conservative, Enough is enough. I'm glad to see the Trump administration is taking action to combat the rising tide of left-wing violence in America. After years of witnessing Antifa members incite and commit violence against innocent people, attack law enforcement officials, and torch federal and private property, President Trump officially designated Antifa as a domestic terrorist organization. This is a move The Heritage Foundation's scholars have promoted since 2019. It will prioritize attention and federal resources on Antifa and provide tools for stopping the funding that allows Antifa to operate. Antifa is not just "a set of ideas." It is an organization with a logo, trained activists, funding, and uses pre-made signs and pre-gathered vehicles, gear, and projectiles. This is a great first step. But there is even more President Trump can do to protect Americans from leftist violence. The Heritage Foundation is urging the FBI to designate Transgender Ideology-Inspired Violent Extremism (TIVE) as domestic terrorism, following a string of attacks by trans or trans-supporting criminals. Especially in the aftermath of the Charlie Kirk assassination, this action is long overdue. If you want to help erase the threat of Antifa, please consider activating your Heritage Foundation membership today. Your tax-deductible gift will help create and promote public policies that will help defeat the threat of violence from Antifa and transgender extremism. It just takes a moment. Our future rests in your hands, Patriot. I wrote this email to give you an update and to make a plea from the heart. _This is a humble request from the heart, so _please_ read it all the way through._In 2021, my team and I put together a historic victory to help flip every one of Virginia's highest offices RED! Ever since taking office as Lieutenant Governor, I have been notching big wins for my friends, neighbors, and every one of my fellow Virginians.Now, I'm running for Governor so that we can keep up the amazing work we've been doing here in the Commonwealth and make history once again.**Unfortunately, I 'm facing a few _SERIOUS_ challenges right now:**
---
**While I was in the Virginia legislative session, I was _legally_ barred from accepting even a dime in support of my campaign. **But my Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, was fundraising _**BIG TIME**_ while I've been working hard in our capital. **Seriously,_a Soros-backed, far-Left group dumped $500K into Abigail Spanberger 's campaign coffers and there wasn't a single thing I could do to respond!_**
---
Now Spanberger has a _**GIGANTIC**_ fundraising advantage, Patriot. Polls show that my race is tied, but Spanberger has a _serious_ cash advantage. name: Rep. Ilhan Omar
subject: Israel is violating the ceasefire in Gaza.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
These actions jeopardize any chance for a lasting peace.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-oct25-gaza/?source=em251020-1-180&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)
Friend, when I shared the news of the ceasefire in Gaza with you last week, I warned that we must stay vigilant until it’s the real deal.
The Palestinians who live in Gaza have suffered for two long years, and have finally been given the opportunity to return to the rubble where their homes once stood, recover their lost friends and family from the wreckage, and attempt to rebuild from the ground up.
But over the weekend we saw exactly how serious Israel is treating this ceasefire. The IDF bombed over a hundred locations in Gaza, killing dozens of people. Dozens more have been shot by IDF soldiers. Humanitarian aid entering Gaza has been even further restricted by Netanyahu and his government. These actions jeopardize any chance for a lasting peace.
Meanwhile, attacks by Israeli settlers against Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank have intensified. Settlers are now emboldened by the focus on Gaza to continue to steal Palestinian land and homes through violent force.
On Friday a 10 year old Palestinian boy was shot dead after the IDF opened fire on a group of kids playing soccer. Yesterday a mob of masked settlers attacked a Palestinian village and chased down an American journalist who was documenting the scene, nearly killing him. The same mob beat an elderly Palestinian woman on the head with a club, sending her to the ICU in critical condition.
Whether it’s violence and theft in the West Bank or the genocide in Gaza, Israel’s policies and military goals continue to be driven by far-right extremists who will stop at nothing short of the complete annexation of Palestine into Israel and the expulsion or even elimination of the Palestinian people.
That’s exactly why we must continue to build the movement for Palestinian justice and self-determination, pressure Congress to block any and all military and economic aid to Israel, and help keep international attention on Israel and their actions.
So, if you are outraged by Israel’s continued attacks and are looking to take action right now, I have one quick way you can help today:
**[Add your name to my petition calling on my fellow members of Congress to support an embargo on aid to Israel until a permanent ceasefire is respected and Israel finally addresses illegal settlements and settler violence in the West Bank. The United States cannot and should not reward a violent, criminal, out-of-control Israeli government with continued financial support.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-oct25-gaza/?source=em251020-1-180&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/em-oct25-gaza/?source=em251020-1-180&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)**
In solidarity,
Ilhan Omar

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=2&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=3&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=4&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=1905_791427_SUPod3&akid=1905%2E791427%2ESUPod3)**.
Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

body:
Did you get this? | | Friends,

We wanted to make sure you knew about our major fundraising deadline tonight at midnight. It’s essential we hit our goals so we can power through to victory on Election Day. **[Can we count on you to rush $25, $50, or whatever you can so we can win in 15 days?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117656407/577197617/-1414184198?contactdata=+y1RH6EDPeJbXWA8/2CorMsuMTz0yhPvJRajKNy5hA0uqsVaond/C9wBdovBiGp3Hz3zAZqlVbFE5OPP67cIb2TSKkmEk24pOwGTVk3UvAk=&am=25.00&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2IxYjYxOGMtZjNhZC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=C7G5a5UlB0VYkG9t--pqHsBmC-cO3B16Yoa3dDVS_Dw=&emci=558f5ce6-daad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=7b1b618c-f3ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=17551891)**
Thanks,

Sean Gregory \---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Team Koslow
Date: Sun, Oct 19, 2025, 5:37 PM
Subject: Will you donate before our deadline tomorrow?
To: Sean Gregory
Sean,

We’re now nearly two weeks out from Election Day, and **tomorrow night’s fundraising deadline couldn’t be more important**.

**[Can you rush $25 today to help us hit our goals and power through to Election Day?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117656408/577197618/-1414184198?contactdata=+y1RH6EDPeJbXWA8/2CorMsuMTz0yhPvJRajKNy5hA0uqsVaond/C9wBdovBiGp3Hz3zAZqlVbFE5OPP67cIb2TSKkmEk24pOwGTVk3UvAk=&am=25.00&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2IxYjYxOGMtZjNhZC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=C7G5a5UlB0VYkG9t--pqHsBmC-cO3B16Yoa3dDVS_Dw=&emci=558f5ce6-daad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=7b1b618c-f3ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=17551891)**

Seth and the rest of the team are knocking on doors, talking to voters, and getting our message out to fight back against the ridiculous lies being spread across the county, but we need your help to keep the momentum going into November.

**[Can we count on you to help us hit our deadlines so we can hold Bruce Blakeman accountable and make Nassau safer and more affordable?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117656409/577197619/-1414184198?contactdata=+y1RH6EDPeJbXWA8/2CorMsuMTz0yhPvJRajKNy5hA0uqsVaond/C9wBdovBiGp3Hz3zAZqlVbFE5OPP67cIb2TSKkmEk24pOwGTVk3UvAk=&am=25.00&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTYyMTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2IxYjYxOGMtZjNhZC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=C7G5a5UlB0VYkG9t--pqHsBmC-cO3B16Yoa3dDVS_Dw=&emci=558f5ce6-daad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=7b1b618c-f3ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=17551891)**

Lets win this,
Team Koslow

body:
The president's net approval rating is -15%. Here's what this means:
#
Need a little bit of good news to start your week, Peter?
**Donald Trump’s approval rating remains extremely low among everyday Americans.**
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96807?refcode=em20251020-9603&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9603%2E614464%2Em13yaM)
**[BUILD ON THIS MOMENTUM: CHIP IN](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96807?refcode=em20251020-9603&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9603%2E614464%2Em13yaM)**
Here’s what this means: **The majority of Americans do not approve of Donald Trump’s agenda.**
They have felt the cost of living get higher and higher, all while GOP politicians make life easier for the billionaires bankrolling their campaigns.
They have seen how the GOP has shamelessly stripped away fundamental rights and actively worked to rig our elections in their favor.
_And they know these attacks on everyday Americans won’t stop here._
That’s why we’ve seen Democratic candidates steadily gain momentum since the beginning of this year — in red and blue states alike! **People are more motivated than ever to take our country back from Donald Trump + the GOP.**
**So today, we’re asking for your help to take advantage of that energy, Peter.[Will you help us elect Democrats in all 50 states and build on the momentum we’re seeing for Democrats across the country by making a donation of $5 or whatever you can today?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96807?refcode=em20251020-9603&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9603%2E614464%2Em13yaM)**

clean_body: The president's net approval rating is -15%. Here's what this means: Need a little bit of good news to start your week, Peter? Donald Trump’s approval rating remains extremely low among everyday Americans. Here’s what this means: The majority of Americans do not approve of Donald Trump’s agenda. They have felt the cost of living get higher and higher, all while GOP politicians make life easier for the billionaires bankrolling their campaigns. They have seen how the GOP has shamelessly stripped away fundamental rights and actively worked to rig our elections in their favor. And they know these attacks on everyday Americans won’t stop here. That’s why we’ve seen Democratic candidates steadily gain momentum since the beginning of this year — in red and blue states alike! People are more motivated than ever to take our country back from Donald Trump + the GOP. So today, we’re asking for your help to take advantage of that energy, Peter. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: Thank you. — State Democrats HQ Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats.com). name: Defend Our Courts
subject: WTF is happening in the Pennsylvania Supreme Court Race
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
Why is an obscure judicial race getting so much attention. If MAGA seizes this court they all but guarantee Trump will take Pennsylvania and its critical 19 electoral college votes in 2028
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iQC8bcICjt5NrNk9mPqsazQwlIxa9zThXttoQ8ml0LUYPKajevShJlp5GovHwyKPOjkmjS2ceQZcRFfZRb2UkTAR5lOqlAjdm8c7vg5E0soe335NajqUKjJOAh97-Xf-v0GqUSBGatMeoIaX4Kx1XfH3rowSlRsy7dsZOPzdZCd1zZJ4sfBmdXwEQW57ICHRXdxw5Ikqm8UTDYp6T7ao4PTtTthCVWfq&c=-sD4slxiXYHhvsEsdHd1YQ4HLT2-UqdwtEgZZhBkq3aQhXBYFNT-ow==&ch=mF2KMlE4WXSIJ7x786RciouNo1pM2qDMd2kKdY6W5jAk6K9BAhjytQ==)
---
| Peter,
The situation on the ground in the Pennsylvania Supreme Court 2025 has gone thermonuclear and is now the hottest election of 2025.
If you are thinking wtf why is an obscure judicial race getting so much attention--we get it. This race matters because if MAGA seizes this court they all but guarantee Trump will take Pennsylvania and its critical 19 electoral college votes in 2028 when he seeks a third term.
Here's the rundown of where things presently stand:
First: In early September MAGA launches a bolt from the blue surprise attack rapidly spending millions in this quiet and often overlooked judicial race and quickly seizes the lead.
Then: Dem PA Governor Shapiro, Defend our Courts, DNC & DLCC, launch a desperate counter-offensive in Late September to pull Democrats back from the brink of defeat.
Now: Things have stabilized and we have somewhat contained MAGA's breakthrough but voters are still confused, the majority remain undecided.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iQC8bcICjt5NrNk9mPqsazQwlIxa9zThXttoQ8ml0LUYPKajevShJlp5GovHwyKPOjkmjS2ceQZcRFfZRb2UkTAR5lOqlAjdm8c7vg5E0soe335NajqUKjJOAh97-Xf-v0GqUSBGatMeoIaX4Kx1XfH3rowSlRsy7dsZOPzdZCd1zZJ4sfBmdXwEQW57ICHRXdxw5Ikqm8Ud1xrl7D3j2mrc6R0ZKrxm&c=-sD4slxiXYHhvsEsdHd1YQ4HLT2-UqdwtEgZZhBkq3aQhXBYFNT-ow==&ch=mF2KMlE4WXSIJ7x786RciouNo1pM2qDMd2kKdY6W5jAk6K9BAhjytQ==)
---
The ballots that voters will vote on in this race don't have party affiliation to simplify it for voters. They simply say vote yes or no to retain these justices and leave it up to voters to figure out which is the GOP and Democratic position.
MAGA is taking advantage of this and is spending millions on deceptive ads and mailers to try to convince Democratic and Independent voters to vote no and remove their Supreme Court Justices.
15 days remain until election day and we need to do everything we can to convince our undecided voters to vote yes. [Please, can you make a donation of whatever you can spare so we can rush it to the frontlines and put it to work convincing these undecided voters to vote yes?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001iQC8bcICjt5NrNk9mPqsazQwlIxa9zThXttoQ8ml0LUYPKajevShJlp5GovHwyKPYHJa1_oTzyAXWLQqIQjmM2kPJtJdZwkz5c7IlVzeZjyMSdaAov1gsxKu5mQqg2g9qGAOfvoZwzNPhe0YtyH1ztaYkkiVZ48dUxf6Vo5gX3o_hMSSwr-dAOmXppiebE03qF1Wz_344AzJV9XBUiESGooOJe8dQ_KZI1buA8Qa7gs=&c=-sD4slxiXYHhvsEsdHd1YQ4HLT2-UqdwtEgZZhBkq3aQhXBYFNT-ow==&ch=mF2KMlE4WXSIJ7x786RciouNo1pM2qDMd2kKdY6W5jAk6K9BAhjytQ==)

Peter, Racist and homophobic slurs, bragging about sexual assault, and even claiming to "love Hitler." Those are just some of the horrific things leaders of the New York Young Republicans freely said to each other in private chats. No group was spared. Unfortunately, Peter, this doesn’t come as much of a surprise. These days, this is the culture from the top-down in the MAGA Republican Party. These are the same New York Young Republicans that honored GOP Rep. Elise Stefanik earlier this year as "Member of Congress of the Year." The same group that Stefanik called "the backbone" of the New York GOP. This is the face of today’s Republican Party — racist, homophobic, antisemitic, and violent.**That’s not our New York. That’s not America.** New York Republicans like Stefanik may talk a good game publicly, but in truth they don't care about New Yorkers. **[Now more than ever, your voice and support are essential. name: AZ Dems
subject: When will Arizona Republicans condemn this?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY
body:
Rep. Gillette called for a congresswoman to be hanged. Arizona Republicans won't condemn him. That's unacceptable. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117657276/577200636/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251020_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjRjNTAxYjgtZjZhZC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=7J0ibiK8AI2hra4s9z2I26gJ9UE4_ovylvdn3AZfX7Y=)
---
Hi there, **Democrats in the Arizona House of Representatives say they feel unsafe around one of their Republican colleagues.** That's not hyperbole. That's not partisan exaggeration. That's the reality when a lawmaker calls for a sitting congresswoman to be "tried, convicted, and hanged" — and his party's leadership refuses to condemn it. State Rep. John Gillette, publicly stated that Congresswoman Pramila Jayapal should be executed for organizing nonviolent resistance training. When called out, he doubled down, saying she should face a firing squad instead. Let's be clear: Jayapal was teaching people about labor strikes and peaceful protests. Gillette responded by calling for her death. **Arizona's Democratic state representatives wrote to Republican House leaders demanding accountability. Their response? Silence.** House Speaker Steve Montenegro won't comment. Ethics Committee Chair Lupe Diaz won't take action. Arizona Republicans are betting they can ignore calls for political violence without consequences. They're wrong. [**Will you support our fight for accountability for dangerous rhetoric that threatens public servants and erodes democratic norms?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117657277/577200637/541746778?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251020_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjRjNTAxYjgtZjZhZC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=7J0ibiK8AI2hra4s9z2I26gJ9UE4_ovylvdn3AZfX7Y=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117657278/577200638/79744751?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251020_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjRjNTAxYjgtZjZhZC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=7J0ibiK8AI2hra4s9z2I26gJ9UE4_ovylvdn3AZfX7Y=)
---
Arizona deserves leaders who condemn threats against public servants regardless of party. Right now, we're getting Republican leadership that enables extremism and looks the other way when their own members threaten violence.
We're not letting this slide. And we're making sure Arizona voters know exactly who's protecting extremists instead of condemning them.
Thanks for standing with us,
Arizona Democratic Party

name: Team Schrier
subject: An important update on the Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Will you stand with Kim? >> [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117657312/577200842/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWJkNDE1MC1mN2FkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Dv1DPaKxClebtaJBy66hv1AJfpZfXkDafJeLkCHm7pw=&emci=d5b19be8-cead-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=bebd4150-f7ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=15820338)
---
**“Schrier letter demands Department of Energy reverse Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub termination” -_NCW Life_**
* * *
Peter,

Earlier this month, the Trump Administration pulled **$1 billion** in clean energy funding from Washington— _money that was supposed to power the Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub._

This project would have created more than 10,000 jobs, brought billions in private investment, and helped secure America's clean energy future. Now, that progress is at risk, and it's all because our president would rather play petty politics than fight for our district.

That's why Kim is fighting tooth and nail to get this funding restored. She signed a letter with other Washington and Pacific Northwest lawmakers to Energy Secretary Chris Wright that denounces the funding cancellation and demands action to get it back.

This project didn’t start off politically; it came from years of teamwork across party lines to make our region a leader in clean energy and good-paying jobs. **But the Trump Administration is making it political****—****these funding cuts in Washington State mirror cuts to more than 200 other similar programs, all in primarily blue-leaning states.**

Peter, Kim's message is clear: Washington deserves better. We need to restore this funding, protect local jobs, and keep America moving forward.

[**If you stand with Kim, add your name now to demand the administration reverse this decision and return the promised investment to Washington immediately.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117657314/577200844/-831927110?ms=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&refcode=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&source=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=ETTTrFAoGPZKhTP3aBLDJOYCpiB4kbmKONstyOLg7iYb50TRbkWF9wVyoyL5xRzPG1sO+gSIQ+Xf3BDEntZdIBQIsI0qOvetdE2w4wH3fK/sbKi3JI8oTA3Eyby3C0Xfz0kouyxC3G+7JbomF1f9xOqpOl8TZe2uuyGb/1zgBck=&refcode2=bebd4150-f7ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWJkNDE1MC1mN2FkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Dv1DPaKxClebtaJBy66hv1AJfpZfXkDafJeLkCHm7pw=&emci=d5b19be8-cead-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=bebd4150-f7ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=15820338) [ADD YOUR NAME TO STAND WITH KIM →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117657316/577200846/-831927110?ms=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&refcode=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&source=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251020_hydhub_adv_gq_act___TS_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=ETTTrFAoGPZKhTP3aBLDJOYCpiB4kbmKONstyOLg7iYb50TRbkWF9wVyoyL5xRzPG1sO+gSIQ+Xf3BDEntZdIBQIsI0qOvetdE2w4wH3fK/sbKi3JI8oTA3Eyby3C0Xfz0kouyxC3G+7JbomF1f9xOqpOl8TZe2uuyGb/1zgBck=&refcode2=bebd4150-f7ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWJkNDE1MC1mN2FkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Dv1DPaKxClebtaJBy66hv1AJfpZfXkDafJeLkCHm7pw=&emci=d5b19be8-cead-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=bebd4150-f7ad-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=15820338) Thank you,

clean_body: Will you stand with Kim? >> “Schrier letter demands Department of Energy reverse Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub termination” -NCW Life Peter, Earlier this month, the Trump Administration pulled $1 billion in clean energy funding from Washington— money that was supposed to power the Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub. This project would have created more than 10,000 jobs, brought billions in private investment, and helped secure America's clean energy future. Now, that progress is at risk, and it's all because our president would rather play petty politics than fight for our district. That's why Kim is fighting tooth and nail to get this funding restored. She signed a letter with other Washington and Pacific Northwest lawmakers to Energy Secretary Chris Wright that denounces the funding cancellation and demands action to get it back. This project didn’t start off politically; it came from years of teamwork across party lines to make our region a leader in clean energy and good-paying jobs. But the Trump Administration is making it political**—these funding cuts in Washington State mirror cuts to more than 200 other similar programs, all in primarily blue-leaning states. Peter, Kim's message is clear: Washington deserves better. We need to restore this funding, protect local jobs, and keep America moving forward. Thank you, Team Schrier Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. | = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. name: Nikki Budzinski
subject: I’m showing up & listening to IL-13
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nikki for Congress
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117658257/577203871/-2063340125?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNjBiNzQyMi1mOGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fW3GAs68x6-o2K-cZ6CcqLtUIR1TIV3L9WeMoDLNXEg=)
Friend,

This is a tough time for IL-13 and communities nationwide as the Republican-led government shutdown drags on. I’m committed to doing right by Central and Southern Illinois – and it starts with showing up and listening.

I’m energized by the folks I’m meeting and taking their stories back to Washington with me as I fight to end the shutdown and ensure affordable and accessible health care.

So today, I wanted to catch you up on some of the events I’ve gone to in the last few weeks. I’m fired up to keep fighting for working families in Illinois – and I hope you’ll read my update the whole way through.

Thank you,

Nikki
| **[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117658259/577203873/-2063340125?refcode=em-10.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNjBiNzQyMi1mOGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fW3GAs68x6-o2K-cZ6CcqLtUIR1TIV3L9WeMoDLNXEg=)**
---

* * *

I was glad to stop by the WSOY Food Drive and discuss how we can make sure everyone in our communities has access to food. This was one of the largest single-day food drives in the nation, collecting over 30 million pounds of food in 20+ years!
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117658260/577203874/-2063340125?refcode=em-10.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNjBiNzQyMi1mOGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fW3GAs68x6-o2K-cZ6CcqLtUIR1TIV3L9WeMoDLNXEg=)
I had a great weekend at the Belleville Chili Cookout, supporting many of our local businesses.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117658262/577203876/-2063340125?refcode=em-10.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNjBiNzQyMi1mOGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fW3GAs68x6-o2K-cZ6CcqLtUIR1TIV3L9WeMoDLNXEg=)
Last month, I visited the Farm Progress Show grounds with the Illinois Soybean Association and learned about how conservation practices support Illinois farmers.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117658263/577203879/-2063340125?refcode=em-10.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNjBiNzQyMi1mOGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fW3GAs68x6-o2K-cZ6CcqLtUIR1TIV3L9WeMoDLNXEg=)
I also joined the Mason County Dems River Party in September to talk about how we can band together to make change happen and protect our communities.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117658264/577203880/-2063340125?refcode=em-10.20.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNjBiNzQyMi1mOGFkLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fW3GAs68x6-o2K-cZ6CcqLtUIR1TIV3L9WeMoDLNXEg=)
I’ll continue to keep pushing at home and back in Washington to stand up for working families and the thousands of Illinoisans under threat during the shutdown. Listening to your stories is a key part of my job and directly informs my work in Congress.

Thank you, and have a great rest of your weekend!

Nikki
